656811.background: Ryan O'Hearn turned into a legitimate fantasy first baseman after spending years as a platoon player, emerging with Baltimore as a contact-first lefty bat who hits the ball hard and draws walks at a useful rate. His transition to a full-time role showcased 20-homer upside with solid on-base skills that fit the Orioles' patient lineup philosophy. In CBA he's a solid floor player who doesn't hurt you in any category.
656811.recentAnalysis: O'Hearn has had a strong start: .313/.408/.485 (0.893 OPS) with 3 HR, 13 RBI, and 10 walks through 19 games — hitting for average, drawing walks, and contributing in counting stats.

671056.name: Ivan Herrera
671056.background: Ivan Herrera took over as the Cardinals' primary catcher and immediately demonstrated elite on-base skills that make him an unusual fantasy weapon at a position known for poor plate discipline. His remarkable walk rates produce strong OBP even when the batting average dips, and his modest power in a home-run-friendly park gives him solid CBA value. Catcher eligibility combined with his patience makes him a coveted deep-league asset.
671056.recentAnalysis: Herrera is walking at a remarkable rate in 2026: 18 BB in 88 PA (.212 AVG, 0.746 OPS) — the average is soft but his extraordinary patience makes him a genuine on-base weapon with 2 HR and 10 RBI adding counting stat value.

669016.name: Brandon Marsh
669016.background: Brandon Marsh is a lengthy, tooled-up outfielder for Philadelphia who combines solid raw power with good speed and exceptional defense in center field. He struggled with strikeouts early in his career but gradually improved his contact rate to become a productive everyday bat in a loaded Phillies lineup with postseason pedigree. In CBA he provides speed, defensive value, and power-upside in a lineup that consistently generates runs.
669016.recentAnalysis: Marsh has had a nice early stretch: .290/.318/.484 with 2 HR, 11 RBI, and 2 SB across 17 games — solid across-the-board production from a bat that benefits from Philadelphia's elite lineup protection.

676391.name: Ernie Clement
676391.background: Ernie Clement has carved out a roster spot as one of the better pure contact hitters in the AL, using a spray approach and exceptional bat-to-ball skills to maintain a high batting average with minimal strikeouts. His lack of power limits his fantasy ceiling, but his ability to hit .290-plus while barely striking out makes him a useful AVG contributor in CBA. His multi-position eligibility and contact skills keep him relevant in keeper formats.
676391.recentAnalysis: Clement is doing what he does: .289/.299/.382 with 0 HR, 5 RBI, and only 5 strikeouts in 77 PA — near-zero power but elite contact skills are on full display in the early going.

601713.name: Nick Pivetta
601713.background: Nick Pivetta has reinvented himself multiple times over his career, most recently becoming one of the Red Sox's more reliable rotation starters with above-average strikeout rates and improved command. His power fastball and late-breaking slider generate swing-and-miss throughout the lineup, and when his mechanics are dialed in he's a legitimate SP2-SP3 in fantasy. He's been a consistent innings-eater and strikeout source for CBA managers.
601713.recentAnalysis: Pivetta is currently on the 15-day IL; he made 4 starts before landing on the IL with 16.0 IP, 4.50 ERA, 1.13 WHIP, and 24 K — the strikeout rate was excellent but he needs to return healthy for his fantasy managers.

690993.name: Colt Keith
690993.background: Colt Keith arrived as one of the Tigers' most polished offensive prospects, showing a mature approach at the plate with a line-drive swing that generates consistent hard contact to all fields. His debut season showcased excellent bat-to-ball skills and the potential for a .290-plus average as he matures into his power. In CBA he's an exciting young keeper who could develop into a .280/20 HR second baseman as Detroit's rebuild matures.
690993.recentAnalysis: Keith has made a strong early impression: .317/.349/.433 with 0 HR but 5 RBI and 10 runs through 18 games — the contact skills are evident and the power figures to come as the season progresses.

670770.name: TJ Friedl
670770.background: TJ Friedl emerged as one of the more dangerous leadoff hitters in the NL for Cincinnati, combining elite contact skills with outstanding stolen base ability and a willingness to work counts. His 2023 breakout season showcased a .300 average with 20-plus steals, and his speed profile makes him a CBA asset in the stolen base category. He's a high-average, high-SB contributor who helps in runs and SB-dependent scoring systems.
670770.recentAnalysis: Friedl has had a brutal start to 2026: just .143/.269/.143 (0.411 OPS) with 0 HR and 1 RBI in 20 games — only 3 SB providing any value while he works through an extended cold stretch at the plate.

669923.name: George Kirby
669923.background: George Kirby has established himself as one of the most command-oriented starting pitchers in baseball, routinely posting walk rates that are among the lowest in the majors while generating consistent strikeouts for Seattle. His mechanical precision and ability to locate his fastball to all four quadrants make him a perpetual WHIP and ERA asset in fantasy, and he rarely has blow-up outings. In CBA he's a rock-solid SP who grades out as one of the most reliable starters in the league.
669923.recentAnalysis: Kirby is doing exactly what he does: 4 GS, 27.2 IP, 3.25 ERA, 0.94 WHIP, 22 K — elite control fully on display with minimal free passes and a fantastic innings total in just 4 outings.

663586.name: Austin Riley
663586.background: Austin Riley has become one of the most powerful third basemen in the National League, posting multiple 30-homer seasons for Atlanta with a pull-heavy approach that generates elite hard-hit rates. His transition from a high-strikeout, high-power profile to a more contact-balanced hitter has made him a legitimate .270-hitting 30-HR threat. In CBA he's one of the most coveted corner infielders and a perennial All-Star vote-getter.
663586.recentAnalysis: Riley has started decently: .240/.321/.413 with 3 HR, 13 RBI, 12 runs, and 2 SB through 20 games — the walks and SB are nice additions to the usual power profile, though the average is running below his norms.

664040.name: Brandon Lowe
664040.background: Brandon Lowe is one of the most dangerous power-hitting second basemen in fantasy baseball when healthy, combining elite launch angles with strong walk rates to produce HR totals that consistently outperform his contact profile. He has battled injuries throughout his career, particularly back issues that have cost him time in multiple seasons, but when available he's a .260/25-HR asset. His CBA value is high when healthy and his power ceiling makes him worth rostering.
664040.recentAnalysis: Lowe is off to a torrid start: .286/.390/.643 (1.033 OPS) with 7 HR and 18 RBI through just 18 games — the power is absolutely exploding and he's walking at an excellent rate to boot.

686613.name: Hunter Brown
686613.background: Hunter Brown emerged as one of Houston's best young starting pitchers, displaying a powerful fastball-slider combination that generates elite strikeout rates and keeps hitters off balance at every count. His development curve has been rapid, and he quickly established himself as a legitimate frontline arm in a Astros rotation known for developing elite starters. CBA managers who snagged him early have been rewarded with ace-level upside at a bargain price.
686613.recentAnalysis: Brown was spectacular before landing on the 15-day IL: 2 GS, 10.2 IP, 0.84 ERA, 1.03 WHIP, 17 K — utterly dominant in his early outings with a 14.3 K/9, and his managers are anxiously awaiting his return.

665833.name: Oneil Cruz
665833.background: Oneil Cruz possesses arguably the most raw power of any shortstop in baseball history, with a 6-foot-7 frame that generates Statcast exit velocities and distances that defy the position. His 2022 debut was one of the most electrifying rookie seasons in recent memory, and despite injury setbacks he's developed into a legitimate 30-HR, 30-SB threat when healthy. In CBA, his combination of stolen bases, home runs, and run production from the top of Pittsburgh's lineup make him one of the most exciting players to own.
665833.recentAnalysis: Cruz is having an electric start to 2026: .313/.376/.594 (0.970 OPS) with 6 HR, 19 RBI, 16 runs, and 9 SB through 20 games — the five-category production is fully live and he's one of the fantasy leaders in the early going.

669127.name: Shea Langeliers
669127.background: Shea Langeliers established himself as one of the best offensive catchers in baseball with Oakland, posting back-to-back 20-plus homer seasons with solid walk rates from behind the plate. His raw power at the catcher position is genuinely rare, and his framing and defense make him a premium defensive asset as well. In CBA, catcher-eligible players with 20-HR potential are extremely scarce, making him one of the most valuable positional assets in the league.
669127.recentAnalysis: Langeliers is having a tremendous start: .320/.398/.600 (0.999 OPS) with 6 HR, 12 RBI, and 14 runs through 19 games — one of the best offensive starts by a catcher in the league and a premium fantasy asset.

669065.name: Kyle Stowers
669065.background: Kyle Stowers is a power-oriented outfielder for Baltimore who has shown flashes of big HR upside with a pull-heavy approach and excellent bat speed. His development into a semi-regular role has been gradual, but the raw power tool and Orioles lineup support give him legitimate upside as a keeper. In CBA he's a speculative hold for managers expecting his plus-power to eventually translate into regular production.
669065.recentAnalysis: Stowers is currently on the 10-day IL and has not appeared in a 2026 game yet — his managers are waiting for his return before evaluating his early-season contributions.

676440.name: Tanner Bibee
676440.background: Tanner Bibee was one of the best pitching prospects in baseball before his callup with Cleveland, showcasing exceptional command, a plus changeup, and the ability to generate weak contact that defies his modest velocity. His MLB debut was stellar, earning him early Cy Young consideration, and he's established himself as one of the cornerstones of Cleveland's excellent rotation. In CBA he's a high-value SP with excellent ratios and a long projected career as an above-average fantasy starter.
676440.recentAnalysis: Bibee has struggled in his first 5 starts: 24.1 IP, 4.81 ERA, 1.52 WHIP, 23 K — more walks and hard contact than his usual profile, though the strikeout rate remains solid at 8.5 K/9.

621020.name: Dansby Swanson
621020.background: Dansby Swanson brought a championship pedigree to Chicago after winning a World Series ring with Atlanta, immediately proving his value with 20-plus homers, strong defense, and excellent on-base skills from the shortstop position. His ability to walk at an above-average rate while generating 20-25 homers in Chicago's lineup makes him a reliable CBA producer, and his Gold Glove-caliber defense ensures he'll play every day. He's been a consistent mid-range value for CBA teams.
621020.recentAnalysis: Swanson is struggling at the plate in 2026: .210/.354/.368 (0.773 OPS) with 4 HR, 12 RBI, and 19 runs through 19 games — the walks (15 BB) and run scoring are excellent but the batting average is lagging significantly.

678662.name: Ezequiel Tovar
678662.background: Ezequiel Tovar has established himself as one of the better young shortstops in the NL, showing excellent defense with developing offensive tools that project toward a .270/20-HR ceiling. His aggressive approach at the plate generates some strikeouts but also solid contact when he's locked in, and Colorado's elevation gives him a homer-friendly environment. In CBA he's a solid mid-tier SS with multi-category upside as he continues to mature.
678662.recentAnalysis: Tovar has had a modest start: .237/.263/.329 (0.657 OPS) with 1 HR, 6 RBI through 20 games — the contact has been inconsistent and the power hasn't shown up yet in the early going.

592885.name: Christian Yelich
592885.background: A cornerstone of the West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls outfield, Christian Yelich won the 2018 NL MVP with one of the most dominant offensive seasons in Brewers history — .326/36 HR/22 SB — and remains a .280-caliber hitter with 20-homer upside in Milwaukee's lineup. Injuries and some mechanical adjustments reduced his production in subsequent years, but the Pepperoni Rolls have held his rights banking on a return toward that elite form. When healthy, there are few more dangerous outfielders on any CBA roster.
592885.recentAnalysis: Yelich is currently on the 10-day IL; he appeared in 15 games before the injury, hitting .314/.375/.451 with 1 HR, 10 RBI, 10 runs, and 3 SB — a promising start before going down.

543807.name: George Springer
543807.background: A prized veteran on the Ghent Whistlepigs roster, George Springer is a former World Series MVP and five-time All-Star who brings elite leadoff skills, 25-plus homer power, and legitimate stolen base speed. His transition to Toronto was followed by multiple injury-shortened seasons, but when healthy he remains a top-20 outfielder with the full five-category profile. The Whistlepigs have kept faith through his injury history because the ceiling remains undeniably elite.
543807.recentAnalysis: Springer is on the 10-day IL after appearing in just 14 games (.185 AVG, 2 HR, 6 RBI) — limited production before the injury and Ghent is waiting for a healthy return.

662139.name: Daulton Varsho
662139.background: One of the Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets' most versatile weapons, Daulton Varsho holds both catcher and outfield eligibility while contributing 20-plus home runs and solid stolen base numbers. His ability to play multiple positions while maintaining fantasy-relevant production makes him exceptionally valuable in CBA's positional structure, and the Ferrets have leveraged his dual eligibility to stay flexible on roster construction. Toronto's lineup provides solid run-scoring opportunities to back up his solid bat.
662139.recentAnalysis: Varsho has made a solid early showing: .262/.354/.433 with 3 HR, 7 RBI, and 1 SB through 19 games — steady multi-category contributions from one of fantasy's most flexible positional assets.

671218.name: Heliot Ramos
671218.background: A raw power outfielder on the Manhattan Mega Rats roster, Heliot Ramos emerged from the Giants' system with elite bat speed and strength that generated exciting exit velocity numbers in his first extended MLB opportunities. His early-career profile features boom-or-bust at-bats with a high strikeout rate, but the hard contact when he connects is genuinely loud. The Mega Rats are holding his rights as a developmental keep, betting on his tools clicking together into a true five-category contributor.
671218.recentAnalysis: Ramos has had a slow start: .232/.274/.304 (0.578 OPS) with 1 HR and 11 RBI through 19 games — 24 strikeouts in 73 PA is the defining number, and the power the Mega Rats are waiting for hasn't materialized yet.

621043.name: Carlos Correa
621043.background: A franchise-level acquisition for the Delmarva Emus, Carlos Correa is a former World Series champion and ALCS MVP who has maintained his status as one of the best offensive shortstops in baseball through consistent 25-homer, 75-RBI seasons with elite on-base skills. His patience at the plate, postseason excellence, and defensive reputation have made him a cornerstone wherever he plays. The Emus count on his steady middle-of-the-lineup production to anchor their CBA roster year after year.
621043.recentAnalysis: Correa has had a solid start: .271/.348/.383 with 1 HR, 9 RBI, and 15 runs through 18 games — excellent run-scoring from a productive spot in Minnesota's lineup, though the power has been quiet early.

575929.name: Willson Contreras
575929.background: The Delmarva Emus' starting catcher and one of their most passionate contributors, Willson Contreras has been one of the most productive catchers in the NL for the better part of a decade — combining 20-plus homer power with excellent arm strength and a take-charge presence behind the plate. His St. Louis tenure has produced continued offensive production in a lineup built around his RBI opportunities. For Delmarva, he's the rare CBA catcher who can legitimately anchor a starting lineup slot.
575929.recentAnalysis: Contreras has made an excellent early showing for the Emus: .283/.421/.500 (0.921 OPS) with 3 HR, 11 RBI, and 11 walks through 18 games — the walks and power are both there in what's been one of the better catcher starts in the league.

642715.name: Willy Adames
642715.background: A power-hitting shortstop and one of the West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls' middle-infield anchors, Willy Adames has consistently produced 20-plus homers from the position across Tampa Bay, Milwaukee, and now San Francisco. His willingness to walk combined with 25-homer potential makes him one of the better offensive shortstops in the NL, and the Pepperoni Rolls have benefited from his reliable production at a position of strength. His new Giants ballpark has proven home run-friendly for right-handed hitters.
642715.recentAnalysis: Adames has gotten off to a nice start with San Francisco: .269/.303/.493 (0.834 OPS) with 3 HR, 6 RBI, and 10 runs through 20 games — the power is showing up and counting stats should build as the season progresses.

673357.name: Luis Robert Jr.
673357.background: One of the Syracuse Sky Chiefs' most dynamic outfielders, Luis Robert Jr. has one of the highest tool ceilings of any player at his position — combining elite speed, a cannon arm, and plus raw power that generated one of the best defensive seasons in recent CF history. His injury history has been the primary obstacle to becoming a perennial top-10 outfielder, but when healthy his 25-HR, 15-SB combination is among the best in CBA. Syracuse has been patient through his IL stints banking on the ceiling.
673357.recentAnalysis: Robert has made a patient start: .242/.365/.363 with 2 HR, 7 RBI, and 2 SB through 18 games — the walk rate (12 BB) is notably improved from previous seasons, though the power production the Sky Chiefs are counting on hasn't fully arrived.

592332.name: Kevin Gausman
592332.background: The ace of the Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets rotation, Kevin Gausman's late-career reinvention around his split-finger fastball has made him one of the most dangerous starters in the NL. He generates elite strikeout rates and weak contact from one of the most effective pitches in the game, and his Giants tenure has been defined by consistent sub-3.50 ERA campaigns with Cy Young-caliber strikeout numbers. For the Ferrets, he's the crown jewel of their staff — a true fantasy ace they can pencil in every fifth day.
592332.recentAnalysis: Gausman has been outstanding in 2026: 4 GS, 22.1 IP, 2.42 ERA, 0.85 WHIP, 31 K — a 12.5 K/9 with almost no free passes is peak Gausman, and the Ferrets are getting full ace value from their top starter.

607043.name: Brandon Nimmo
607043.background: A key piece of the Delmarva Emus outfield, Brandon Nimmo is one of baseball's elite on-base presences — a leadoff hitter whose extraordinary patience and walk rates produce OBP figures consistently above .380. His re-signing with the Mets confirmed his status as a franchise anchor, and he provides solid counting stat accumulation from the top of New York's order with 20-homer upside as a bonus. The Emus rely on his runs, walks, and reliability to help power one of CBA's more stacked rosters.
607043.recentAnalysis: Nimmo is off to a terrific start for Delmarva: .313/.396/.537 (0.934 OPS) with 4 HR, 11 RBI, and 13 runs through 20 games — delivering on his elite OBP profile with added punch in the power department.

695505.name: Chase Burns
695505.background: One of the Sugar Land Space Cowboys' most exciting young arms, Chase Burns arrived as the Reds' most prized pitching prospect with a power arsenal featuring a triple-digit fastball and a devastating slider that generates elite whiff rates. His rapid ascent through Cincinnati's system and immediate MLB success demonstrated that his raw stuff translates at the highest level, and he's quickly become one of the NL's more exciting young starters. The Space Cowboys are betting on him developing into a genuine frontline ace.
695505.recentAnalysis: Burns has made an impressive early statement for Sugar Land: 4 GS, 22.1 IP, 2.42 ERA, 1.07 WHIP, 22 K — the raw stuff is translating into results with solid strikeout numbers and excellent run prevention.

605483.name: Blake Snell
605483.background: The headlining free agent acquisition for the Sugar Land Space Cowboys rotation, Blake Snell is a two-time Cy Young winner whose electric stuff — elite velocity, a hammer curveball, and devastating command when locked in — has produced some of the most dominant pitching performances in recent memory. His 2023 Giants season was one of the finest individual pitching campaigns this decade before injury interrupted his momentum, and Sugar Land is counting on him anchoring their staff when healthy. The ceiling justifies every bit of the roster investment.
605483.recentAnalysis: Snell is currently on the 15-day IL and has not made a 2026 start yet — the Space Cowboys are waiting anxiously for their ace to return.

543037.name: Gerrit Cole
543037.background: The anchor of the Syracuse Sky Chiefs' pitching staff and one of the most accomplished right-handed starters of his generation, Gerrit Cole has built a career on elite fastball-slider combinations, exceptional strikeout rates, and multiple Cy Young Award finishes. His 2023 ALCS injury was a setback, but when fully healthy he remains a genuine ace who provides the Sky Chiefs with top-3 SP value. A full season from Cole at the top of Syracuse's rotation is a genuine CBA championship weapon.
543037.recentAnalysis: Cole is on the 15-day IL to open 2026 and has not yet taken the mound — Syracuse is hoping for a swift return from their most important starting pitcher.

678394.name: Brayan Bello
678394.background: A mid-rotation piece for the Bristol Banshees, Brayan Bello emerged as one of the Red Sox's better young starters with a sinker-heavy approach that generates ground balls and weak contact at a high rate. His stuff is not flashy — he won't rack up elite strikeout totals — but his command and pitch mix make him a reliable WHIP and ERA contributor for Boston's rotation. Bristol values him as a steady innings-eater who keeps the team competitive in pitching categories week to week.
678394.recentAnalysis: Bello has had a rough start to 2026: 3 GS, 14.2 IP, 6.14 ERA, 1.91 WHIP, 9 K — the control has been off and he's been hit hard in his early outings, which will concern Bristol's pitching staff.

665926.name: Andres Gimenez
665926.background: A versatile middle infield piece for the Bristol Banshees, Andres Gimenez's breakout season with Cleveland established him as one of the best defensive second basemen in the AL while also showcasing offensive skills that surprised many — elite contact, useful power, and surprising stolen base ability. His ability to contribute in multiple offensive categories while playing premium defense has made him a valuable fantasy asset, and Bristol has used his multi-category upside to stay competitive across the board.
665926.recentAnalysis: Gimenez has been solid early for the Banshees: .290/.338/.484 with 3 HR, 11 RBI, and 4 SB through 19 games — solid across-the-board production with the stolen base contributions adding real value to Bristol's weekly totals.

596115.name: Trevor Story
596115.background: A high-upside gamble on the Portland Chinook roster, Trevor Story was one of the most dangerous shortstops in baseball during his Colorado years — combining 30-plus homers with 25-plus steals in a five-category profile that made him perennially top-10 at his position. His Red Sox tenure has been marred by injuries including Tommy John surgery, but Portland has held his rights banking on a return to that elite form. When healthy, Story remains one of the best power-speed shortstops CBA has ever seen.
596115.recentAnalysis: Story has had a very tough start for Portland: .198/.223/.311 (0.533 OPS) with 2 HR and 17 RBI through 19 games — 26 strikeouts in 85 PA is alarming, and the Chinook are still waiting for the breakout they drafted him for.

668227.name: Randy Arozarena
668227.background: A popular piece of the Portland Chinook outfield, Randy Arozarena became a folk hero during the 2020 postseason with a record-setting performance, then validated that debut with back-to-back 20-HR, 20-SB seasons for Tampa Bay before his trade to Seattle. His combination of power, speed, and clutch performance has made him a fantasy mainstay, and Portland relies on his stolen base contributions and run-scoring to stay competitive in those categories. His Mariners move suppresses some power but the speed travels anywhere.
668227.recentAnalysis: Arozarena has made a steady start for the Chinook: .284/.371/.392 with 1 HR, 7 RBI, 16 runs, and 5 SB through 21 games — the stolen base contributions are excellent and the OBP skills are showing up nicely.

593428.name: Xander Bogaerts
593428.background: A cornerstone of the Portland Chinook infield, Xander Bogaerts is a two-time World Series champion and perennial All-Star who built his reputation as one of the most consistent offensive shortstops in baseball over a decade in Boston before signing a massive deal with San Diego. His elite contact skills, gap power, and on-base ability have consistently produced .280-plus averages with 20-plus homers, and Portland has benefited from his steady production as a middle-infield anchor for multiple CBA seasons.
593428.recentAnalysis: Bogaerts has had a solid start for Portland: .260/.325/.411 with 3 HR, 13 RBI, and 2 SB through 19 games — consistent contact and counting stats with his usual elite bat-to-ball skills keeping strikeouts minimal.

663728.name: Cal Raleigh
663728.background: The power-hitting catcher for the Portland Chinook, Cal Raleigh set the MLB home run record for the position in 2022 and has repeatedly demonstrated plus raw power from behind the plate. His willingness to swing for the fences creates a massive strikeout profile, but the home run production makes him one of the few catchers who can anchor a lineup slot. Portland values him enormously as a catcher-eligible player with genuine 25-HR upside — a scarcity in CBA that's difficult to replace.
663728.recentAnalysis: Raleigh has had a rough start at the plate for the Chinook: .163/.275/.253 with 2 HR and 11 RBI through 21 games — 29 strikeouts in 91 PA is a brutal rate, and Portland is waiting for the power that defines his value to show up.

683004.name: Jack Leiter
683004.background: One of the West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls' most exciting young rotation investments, Jack Leiter arrived as one of the most anticipated pitching prospects in years — the son of a MLB veteran with a premium fastball-slider combination and pedigree that generated enormous excitement. His path through the Rangers' system featured strong strikeout numbers, and his MLB debut has shown the raw ability to miss bats at the highest level. West Virginia is betting on continued development from a pitcher with genuine ace upside.
683004.recentAnalysis: Leiter has had a rocky start for the Pepperoni Rolls: 4 GS, 20.1 IP, 4.87 ERA, 1.48 WHIP, 24 K — the strikeout rate is impressive at 10.6 K/9 but walks and hard contact have pushed the ERA above acceptable levels in his early outings.

668804.name: Bryan Reynolds
668804.background: A reliable outfield piece for the Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets, Bryan Reynolds is one of the most underrated outfielders in baseball — a switch-hitter who consistently posts .270-plus averages with 20-plus homers and excellent walk rates for Pittsburgh. His patient approach and ability to hit to all fields make him a model of offensive efficiency, and Ft. Meade has relied on his steady production to stay competitive in average, runs, and power categories year after year.
668804.recentAnalysis: Reynolds has made a solid start for the Ferrets: .264/.379/.449 with 3 HR, 12 RBI, 14 runs, and 13 walks through 20 games — the walks are excellent and he's accumulating counting stats in a productive Pittsburgh lineup.

694819.name: Jacob Misiorowski
694819.background: The Portland Chinook's most electrifying young arm, Jacob Misiorowski features a mid-to-upper-90s fastball combined with a devastating slider that generates historically elite swing-and-miss rates. His rapid development has drawn comparisons to some of the best recent pitching prospects, and Portland secured him as a keeper anticipating exactly this kind of ace emergence. In a rotation that also includes Story-era acquisitions, Misiorowski represents the Chinook's long-term pitching future.
694819.recentAnalysis: Misiorowski has been electric in his early MLB starts for Portland: 4 GS, 21.2 IP, 3.32 ERA, 1.02 WHIP, 33 K — a 13.7 K/9 rate is elite by any measure and he's immediately delivering on the massive prospect hype that convinced the Chinook to keep him.

656305.name: Matt Chapman
656305.background: A veteran presence anchoring the Ghent Whistlepigs corner infield, Matt Chapman is one of the best defensive third basemen in baseball history — a perennial Gold Glove winner who pairs elite glovework with legitimate 25-homer pop and solid on-base skills. His time across Oakland, Toronto, and San Francisco has consistently delivered 20-plus homers and strong defensive ratings, and Ghent has relied on his steady contribution to stay competitive in home runs and RBI over multiple CBA seasons.
656305.recentAnalysis: Chapman has had a solid start for the Whistlepigs: .291/.333/.405 with 1 HR, 11 RBI, and 11 runs through 20 games — consistent contact and counting stat contributions from a reliable veteran presence.

621493.name: Taylor Ward
621493.background: A patient, high-OBP bat for the Portland Chinook outfield, Taylor Ward emerged as a late-blooming hitter who maximized his power and on-base skills after changing his approach at the plate. His ability to work counts and draw walks at an above-average rate gives him a strong OBP profile, and he's shown legitimate 20-homer upside in his best seasons. Portland values him as a reliable floor play who won't hurt you in any category and contributes in runs and walks.
621493.recentAnalysis: Ward has had a nice early stretch for the Chinook: .291/.404/.430 with 0 HR but 13 walks and 10 runs through 20 games — the OBP approach is excellent and the power figures to arrive as the season progresses.

606192.name: Teoscar Hernandez
606192.background: A power bat in the Delmarva Emus outfield, Teoscar Hernandez has developed into one of the more dangerous right-handed hitters in the NL, combining 30-plus homer upside with a high-energy approach that generates big run-scoring opportunities. His time with Toronto, Seattle, and now the Dodgers has demonstrated consistent power production in any environment, and the Emus are benefiting from his placement in one of baseball's best lineups. In a stacked Delmarva roster, Hernandez provides the power punch that completes their outfield.
606192.recentAnalysis: Hernandez has been productive early for the Emus: .286/.324/.524 with 4 HR, 12 RBI, and 12 runs through 17 games — the power is showing up right on schedule in one of baseball's most productive lineups.

668678.name: Zac Gallen
668678.background: A reliable mid-rotation starter for the Syracuse Sky Chiefs, Zac Gallen has been one of the better groundball pitchers in the NL for the Diamondbacks, combining exceptional command with a five-pitch arsenal that keeps hitters off balance throughout games. He was a key contributor to Arizona's surprise World Series run and demonstrated big-game ability as well as regular-season consistency. Syracuse counts on his durability and steady ratios to anchor the middle of their staff week to week.
668678.recentAnalysis: Gallen has had a mixed early stretch for the Sky Chiefs: 5 GS, 25.2 IP, 3.51 ERA — the ERA is solid but a 1.48 WHIP and only 14 K suggests some elevated contact rates early in the season.

669236.name: Jeremiah Jackson
669236.background: A high-upside power hitter for the West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls, Jeremiah Jackson arrived with enormous raw power — one of the loudest exit velocities among prospect hitters — and the Pepperoni Rolls secured him as a keeper anticipating a breakout. His early MLB exposure featured the boom-or-bust profile typical of plus-power prospects, but the home run ceiling is genuinely elite. West Virginia is betting this is the year his bat-to-ball skills catch up to his prodigious strength.
669236.recentAnalysis: Jackson has shown real pop for the Pepperoni Rolls early on: .302/.302/.577 (0.879 OPS) with 5 HR and 17 RBI through 19 games — though 0 walks in 65 PA is an extreme approach that will need to be addressed.

678246.name: Miguel Vargas
678246.background: A versatile infield piece on the Ghent Whistlepigs roster, Miguel Vargas is a switch-hitter with strong on-base skills and patient plate approach who has worked his way into a regular role after earning his stripes in the Dodgers' deep system. His ability to play multiple infield positions gives Ghent roster flexibility, and his walk rates and line-drive contact profile project toward a solid .270 average with gap power as he matures. The Whistlepigs are waiting for that offensive potential to fully click.
678246.recentAnalysis: Vargas has had a tough start for Ghent: .153/.264/.333 (0.593 OPS) with 2 HR and 3 SB across 20 games — the walks are there (12 BB) but the batting average has been extremely poor through 87 PA.

663604.name: Brandon Lockridge
663604.background: A speed-first outfielder on the North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms roster, Brandon Lockridge brings elite base-stealing ability and a scrappy approach at the plate that makes him a specialist in the stolen base and run-scoring categories. The Fuzzy Bottoms have leaned on his speed to stay competitive in those categories, and his value is concentrated in situations where he gets on base and immediately becomes a threat on the basepaths. He won't hurt you in AVG if deployed correctly.
663604.recentAnalysis: Lockridge has contributed what the Fuzzy Bottoms need from him: 4 SB in 17 games with a .255 average and 5 runs through 58 PA — the speed is real even if the power profile (.627 OPS) is limited.

696100.name: Hunter Goodman
696100.background: A power-hitting catcher on the Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets roster, Hunter Goodman emerged from the Rockies' system with one of the better raw power tools among catching prospects, generating impressive exit velocities and home run distances in his early MLB opportunities. The Ferrets secured him as a developmental catcher keep with the upside of a 25-HR backstop if his contact skills develop. At Coors Field he has one of the best home run-friendly environments in baseball to help him post counting stats.
696100.recentAnalysis: Goodman has shown the power the Ferrets are counting on: .257/.321/.527 with 5 HR and 8 RBI through 20 games — though 31 strikeouts in 78 PA is an extremely elevated rate that will determine his ultimate ceiling.

701358.name: Cam Smith
701358.background: One of the most exciting young hitters on the Syracuse Sky Chiefs roster, Cam Smith arrived as one of the top position player prospects in baseball with a polished approach and plus bat speed that generated immediate comparisons to elite contact hitters. His ability to work counts, hit for average, and project toward 20-plus homer power makes him a potential franchise cornerstone for Syracuse. The Sky Chiefs locked him up as a keeper anticipating that this is only the beginning of a long career as a CBA impact bat.
701358.recentAnalysis: Smith has had a promising start for Syracuse: .257/.352/.449 (0.802 OPS) with 3 HR, 10 RBI, 13 runs, and 3 SB across 22 games — solid early contributions across multiple categories from a player who is still just scratching his ceiling.

657757.name: Gavin Sheets
657757.background: A left-handed power bat on the Manhattan Mega Rats roster, Gavin Sheets provides a patient approach and platoon-friendly profile that generates solid home run production against right-handed pitching. His ability to drive the ball into the gaps and draw walks makes him a useful lineup piece for the Mega Rats, and his first base/DH eligibility gives them flexibility in constructing their roster. Manhattan has used him as a reliable counting-stat contributor who won't hurt them in walks.
657757.recentAnalysis: Sheets has had a modest start for the Mega Rats: .238/.273/.381 with 2 HR, 6 RBI, and 8 runs through 19 games — respectable contributions but the walk rate and power have been below his usual profile so far.

661388.name: William Contreras
661388.background: The starting catcher for the Ghent Whistlepigs and one of their more consistent offensive contributors, William Contreras has established himself as a legitimate offensive catcher with a strong arm and above-average hit tool. His ability to maintain a .290-plus average with 20-homer upside makes him one of the better CBA catchers, and Ghent benefits enormously from a backstop who actively helps in batting average and on-base rate rather than being a liability. He and Matt Chapman give Whistlepigs one of the better veteran cores in the league.
661388.recentAnalysis: Contreras has been excellent for the Whistlepigs early: .306/.398/.444 (0.870 OPS) with 2 HR, 11 RBI, and 11 walks through 18 games — a great start combining average, on-base, and counting stat contributions.

643446.name: Jeff McNeil
643446.background: A veteran contact bat for the Ghent Whistlepigs, Jeff McNeil is a former NL batting champion who brings one of the best pure contact profiles in the NL with a slap-and-gap approach and exceptional strikeout avoidance. His .326 batting title season in 2022 showcased his ability to hit for elite average across a full season, and while his power upside is limited, his batting average contribution is genuinely reliable. The Whistlepigs rely on him to hold up their team batting average and keep their strikeouts manageable.
643446.recentAnalysis: McNeil has had a quiet early stretch for Ghent: .266/.349/.342 with 0 HR and 4 RBI through 20 games — the contact skills are there (only 11 K in 73 PA) but the counting stats haven't materialized yet.

663697.name: Jonathan India
663697.background: A former NL Rookie of the Year on the North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms roster, Jonathan India arrived in the big leagues with an elite approach — drawing walks at a prodigious rate while making consistent contact — and immediately delivered 20-plus homers as Cincinnati's everyday second baseman. He's had injury and consistency issues since that debut, but the Fuzzy Bottoms have held his rights banking on a return to that patient, productive form. His combination of walks, power, and multi-position eligibility has real CBA value.
663697.recentAnalysis: India has had a difficult start for North Georgia: .167/.310/.361 (0.623 OPS) with 2 HR and 8 RBI through 17 games — the walks are showing up (8 BB) but the batting average is well below expectations in the early going.

656876.name: Drew Rasmussen
656876.background: A high-upside rotation piece for the Ghent Whistlepigs, Drew Rasmussen has shown the raw stuff to be a dominant starter — featuring a mid-90s sinker that generates elite groundball rates and a slider that misses bats at a strong clip. He's been limited by Tommy John surgery but when fully healthy he profiles as a frontline arm who could dramatically elevate the Whistlepigs' pitching staff. Ghent has been patient through the rehabilitation knowing what he's capable of when locked in.
656876.recentAnalysis: Rasmussen has been outstanding in his return for Ghent: 4 GS, 19.2 IP, 2.75 ERA, 0.66 WHIP, 20 K — the stuff looks healthy and the elite contact-suppression that defines his best outings is fully present.

641343.name: Jake Bauers
641343.background: A versatile bat on the West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls roster, Jake Bauers brings a well-rounded offensive profile with surprising power upside and solid plate discipline that makes him a useful contributor in CBA's multi-category scoring. His ability to play first base and outfield gives the Pepperoni Rolls positional flexibility, and his power-speed combination at the plate profiles toward 20 HR and double-digit steals when everything is working. West Virginia has valued him as a depth piece with upside.
641343.recentAnalysis: Bauers has been a pleasant surprise for the Pepperoni Rolls: .234/.310/.500 (0.794 OPS) with 5 HR, 13 RBI, 13 runs, and 3 SB through 19 games — the power and speed are both contributing in the early going.

694973.name: Paul Skenes
694973.background: The undisputed ace of the West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls and one of the most celebrated pitching prospects in baseball history, Paul Skenes arrived in Pittsburgh with generational hype and immediately delivered — posting a sub-2.00 ERA in his debut half-season with triple-digit velocity and a truly filthy splinker that baffles the best hitters in the game. The Pepperoni Rolls secured him as a keeper knowing they were getting a franchise arm, and he's already one of the most valuable pitchers in all of CBA.
694973.recentAnalysis: Skenes has been excellent to open 2026 for West Virginia: 5 GS, 22.0 IP, 3.27 ERA, 0.91 WHIP, 23 K — the stuff is the same as advertised and with 3 wins he's already among the league leaders in victories.

696149.name: Bubba Chandler
696149.background: One of the Manhattan Mega Rats' most intriguing young rotation pieces, Bubba Chandler is a two-way prospect who Pittsburgh developed as a starting pitcher after he showed premium arm talent that was too good to pass up. His mid-90s fastball and advanced secondary stuff give him genuine ace upside, and the Mega Rats grabbed him as a keeper betting on his development trajectory continuing upward. If his command refines, he could become one of the better young starters in CBA.
696149.recentAnalysis: Chandler has made a solid start for the Mega Rats: 4 GS, 20.0 IP, 3.15 ERA, 1.30 WHIP, 17 K — the ERA is excellent even if the WHIP suggests some traffic on the basepaths in his early outings.

683146.name: Brett Baty
683146.background: A developmental third base prospect for the West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls, Brett Baty arrived as one of the Mets' best position player prospects with a projectable frame, advanced feel for hitting, and the bat speed to project toward 25-plus homers at maturity. His early MLB opportunities have shown the raw tools but also the growing pains of a young hitter still refining his approach at the highest level. West Virginia is holding his rights as a long-term keeper investment in a player who may not reach his ceiling for another year or two.
683146.recentAnalysis: Baty has had a tough start for the Pepperoni Rolls: .190/.197/.254 (0.467 OPS) with 0 HR and 7 RBI through 18 games — 21 strikeouts in 66 PA with almost no walks is a difficult early profile for the developing third baseman.

686611.name: Dylan Crews
686611.background: A former top overall draft pick on the Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets roster, Dylan Crews arrived with immense pedigree as one of the most polished college hitters in recent draft history — combining elite contact skills with solid power and strong makeup that scouts rave about. The Ferrets secured him as a long-term keeper knowing his ceiling is that of a .290-hitting outfielder with 20-homer upside. His development timeline may be longer than expected but Ft. Meade is committed to the investment.
686611.recentAnalysis: Crews does not currently have 2026 MLB stats on record — he may be in the minors or on a non-standard roster status as the Folksy Ferrets await his callup or roster activation.

514888.name: Jose Altuve
514888.background: A beloved veteran anchor for the Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets, Jose Altuve is a future Hall of Famer and one of the most accomplished second basemen in baseball history — a seven-time All-Star, batting champion, ALCS MVP, and the cornerstone of Houston's dynasty. Even in the latter years of his career he provides elite plate discipline, solid power, and legitimate stolen base contributions from one of the game's most complete offensive profiles. The Ferrets have benefited from his steady excellence over multiple CBA seasons.
514888.recentAnalysis: Altuve has had a solid start for Ft. Meade: .278/.388/.444 (0.862 OPS) with 3 HR, 7 RBI, 16 runs, and 15 walks through 21 games — the elite plate discipline is fully intact and he's scoring runs at an excellent rate.

800048.name: Parker Messick
800048.background: The breakout star of the Sugar Land Space Cowboys pitching staff, Parker Messick arrived as a crafty left-handed starter with a deceptive delivery, excellent command, and the ability to generate weak contact through pitch tunneling and sequencing. His development through the system was noted for his advanced feel for pitching beyond his years, and the Space Cowboys locked him up as a keeper anticipating exactly this kind of dominant debut. He's quickly become one of the most exciting young arms in all of CBA.
800048.recentAnalysis: Messick has been absolutely dominant for Sugar Land to open 2026: 4 GS, 25.2 IP, 1.05 ERA, 0.78 WHIP, 25 K — one of the best early-season pitching lines in the league and an immediate argument for ace consideration.

668909.name: Gavin Williams
668909.background: The ace of the North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms rotation and one of their most prized rotation pieces, Gavin Williams features a devastating fastball-curveball combination that generates elite strikeout rates for Cleveland. His power stuff and ability to miss bats deep into games has made him one of the AL's more exciting young starters, and the Fuzzy Bottoms secured him as a keeper knowing he had genuine top-of-the-rotation upside. North Georgia is getting ace production from one of CBA's more underrated pitching investments.
668909.recentAnalysis: Williams has been exceptional for the Fuzzy Bottoms: 5 GS, 29.2 IP, 2.12 ERA, 1.01 WHIP, 40 K — a stunning 12.1 K/9 with only 10 walks in nearly 30 IP is elite stuff, and he's been one of the best pitchers in CBA to start 2026.

690997.name: Nolan McLean
690997.background: A young rotation piece for the Sugar Land Space Cowboys, Nolan McLean is a high-upside arm who combined raw power stuff with developing command through the system and has pushed his way into a MLB rotation opportunity. His fastball and breaking ball generate strong swing-and-miss rates, and the Space Cowboys view him as part of a long-term rotation core that also includes Chase Burns and Parker Messick. Together they give Sugar Land one of the most exciting young pitching trios in CBA.
690997.recentAnalysis: McLean has been excellent for the Space Cowboys: 4 GS, 23.2 IP, 2.28 ERA, 0.76 WHIP, 28 K — outstanding ratios across the board and he's demonstrating the stuff that convinced Sugar Land to keep him as a long-term rotation piece.

543760.name: Marcus Semien
543760.background: A veteran presence and leader in the Sugar Land Space Cowboys clubhouse, Marcus Semien is a two-time All-Star whose elite defensive metrics at shortstop are matched by consistent 25-30 homer power and reliable run production at the top of a lineup. His Rangers tenure produced back-to-back elite offensive seasons before age began to temper the counting stats, but he remains a useful contributor with excellent durability. The Space Cowboys rely on his professionalism and floor production as part of their veteran core.
543760.recentAnalysis: Semien has had a slow start for Sugar Land: .216/.269/.308 (0.577 OPS) with 1 HR, 8 RBI, and 3 runs through 21 games — the power and run production that defines his value haven't shown up yet in the early going.

680574.name: Matt McLain
680574.background: A developmental shortstop for the Manhattan Mega Rats, Matt McLain arrived as one of Cincinnati's most polished middle infield prospects with excellent contact skills, above-average speed, and the ability to hit for a solid average while providing defensive value. His early career has been interrupted by injury but the Mega Rats have been patient with his development knowing the upside — a .280-hitting shortstop with 15-homer power — is worth the wait. Manhattan views him as part of their long-term middle infield solution.
680574.recentAnalysis: McLain has had a difficult early stretch for the Mega Rats: .183/.345/.239 (0.558 OPS) with 0 HR and 3 RBI through 21 games — the walks are excellent (14 BB) but the contact and power have been absent in the early going.

643289.name: Mauricio Dubon
643289.background: A reliable utility contributor for the North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms, Mauricio Dubon has built a long career on consistent contact, multi-position eligibility, and smart baserunning that helps teams in counting stats without being a liability anywhere. He plays outfield and infield equally well and brings solid batting average to the Fuzzy Bottoms' lineup from a spot where many teams play defensive liabilities. North Georgia values his flexibility and reliability as a veteran contributor who keeps their roster construction options open.
643289.recentAnalysis: Dubon has been one of North Georgia's bright spots early: .315/.346/.493 with 2 HR, 14 RBI, and 11 runs through 20 games — solid contact and counting stat production from a player in an everyday lineup role.

666157.name: Nick Lodolo
666157.background: A key rotation piece for the Delmarva Emus, Nick Lodolo is a tall left-hander for Cincinnati whose deceptive extension and plus fastball-slider combination makes him one of the more difficult pitchers for left-handed hitters to handle. When healthy he profiles as a legitimate SP2 who generates solid strikeout rates and keeps walks manageable. The Emus have been patient through his injury history knowing that a full season of Lodolo pitching in CBA gives them a genuine difference-maker at the back of their rotation.
666157.recentAnalysis: Lodolo is currently on the 15-day IL and has not made a 2026 start yet — Delmarva is waiting for their left-handed rotation anchor to return healthy.

605488.name: Jeffrey Springs
605488.background: A crafty left-handed starter who has rejuvenated his career for the Bristol Banshees, Jeffrey Springs transformed himself from a bullpen piece into one of the better rotation bargains in the AL with Tampa Bay, relying on deception, plus changeup tunneling, and elite command to get outs with well-below-average velocity. His Tommy John surgery cost him 2024, but Bristol held his rights knowing a healthy Springs is a legitimate fantasy SP with elite WHIP and ERA potential. The Banshees are reaping the rewards of that patient investment.
605488.recentAnalysis: Springs has been outstanding in his return for Bristol: 4 GS, 24.2 IP, 1.46 ERA, 0.77 WHIP, 20 K — one of the best early-season ERA lines in CBA and a huge boost for the Banshees' pitching staff.

656302.name: Dylan Cease
656302.background: The ace of the Manhattan Mega Rats rotation and one of the most electric starters in the AL, Dylan Cease combines a triple-digit fastball with one of the best sliders in baseball to generate elite strikeout rates and regularly post double-digit K outings. His San Diego tenure has made him a perennial top-15 SP in all fantasy formats, and the Mega Rats secured him knowing he provides the kind of ceiling arm that wins CBA championships. When Cease is on, no lineup in the league is comfortable facing him.
656302.recentAnalysis: Cease has been dominant for the Mega Rats despite 0 wins: 4 GS, 20.2 IP, 1.74 ERA, 1.26 WHIP, 32 K — a 13.9 K/9 is elite by any measure and he's been one of the best pitchers in early 2026.

695243.name: Mason Miller
695243.background: The closer for the Syracuse Sky Chiefs and one of the most terrifying relievers in baseball, Mason Miller features a mid-to-upper-100s fastball that is routinely among the hardest pitches thrown in any given season — an untouchable heater that generates elite strikeout rates and virtually no hard contact. The Sky Chiefs have one of the most dominant save situations in all of CBA, and Miller's save totals have been a major competitive advantage in their weekly matchups. He is the gold standard for CBA closers.
695243.recentAnalysis: Miller has been absolutely unhittable for Syracuse: 10 G, 10.1 IP, 0.00 ERA, 0.39 WHIP, 25 K, 7 SV — one of the most dominant closer stretches in recent CBA history and an enormous asset for the Sky Chiefs.

543243.name: Sonny Gray
543243.background: A veteran rotation piece for the Portland Chinook, Sonny Gray is a craftsman who has reinvented himself multiple times over a long career — most recently as a groundball-heavy, command-oriented starter whose mature pitch mix and exceptional competitiveness have kept him productive well into his 30s. His Cardinals tenure produced some of the best work of his career, and Portland has relied on his steadiness and durability to provide innings at the top of their rotation. He's the kind of reliable veteran every CBA team covets.
543243.recentAnalysis: Gray has had a decent start for the Chinook: 4 GS, 20.1 IP, 4.43 ERA, 1.28 WHIP, 11 K — the ERA is a touch elevated but his 2 wins have been valuable, and the low strikeout rate reflects his contact-management approach.

553993.name: Eugenio Suarez
553993.background: A power-hitting third baseman for the Bristol Banshees, Eugenio Suarez has been one of the most reliable home run hitters in the NL for nearly a decade — posting six consecutive 20-plus HR seasons with a free-swinging approach that sacrifices average for power. The Banshees have leaned on his pop and RBI production to stay competitive in the power categories, and he provides the kind of 25-30 HR floor that is difficult to replicate at third base in CBA. He bats in the middle of Seattle's lineup with consistent RBI opportunities.
553993.recentAnalysis: Suarez has had a solid early run for Bristol: .263/.329/.434 with 3 HR, 11 RBI, and 9 walks through 22 games — the power is building and his usual production profile looks to be right on track.

693304.name: Nick Gonzales
693304.background: A developing second baseman on the North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms roster, Nick Gonzales arrived as one of Pittsburgh's top offensive prospects with a compact, line-drive swing and strong contact approach that projected toward a .290 average with gap power at maturity. His MLB debut has been gradual, but the Fuzzy Bottoms are holding his rights as a long-term keeper knowing his tools are legitimate. North Georgia views him as a future everyday middle infield piece with a high batting average ceiling.
693304.recentAnalysis: Gonzales has had a modest start for the Fuzzy Bottoms: .258/.286/.345 with 0 HR, 11 RBI through 19 games — solid contact and RBI production but the power hasn't shown up yet in the early going.

673237.name: Yainer Diaz
673237.background: A catcher and first base piece for the Manhattan Mega Rats, Yainer Diaz has established himself as a legitimate offensive backstop with Houston — combining a contact-first approach with solid gap power and the ability to hit in a productive lineup. His multi-position eligibility at C and 1B gives the Mega Rats flexibility in constructing their roster, and his Houston lineup protection means consistent RBI opportunities. Manhattan values him as one of the more productive catcher options available in CBA.
673237.recentAnalysis: Diaz has had a slow start for the Mega Rats: .194/.242/.258 (0.501 OPS) with 1 HR and 9 RBI through 16 games — the contact and production that define his value haven't shown up in the early going.

571448.name: Nolan Arenado
571448.background: A veteran cornerstone and future Hall of Famer on the Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets roster, Nolan Arenado is the greatest defensive third baseman of his generation — a ten-time Gold Glove winner whose elite glovework is matched by consistent 25-30 HR, 90-RBI seasons in the middle of a productive lineup. His Cardinals tenure has been defined by continued offensive excellence and postseason leadership, and Ft. Meade has relied on his floor production to stay competitive in the power and RBI categories year after year.
571448.recentAnalysis: Arenado has had a slow offensive start for the Ferrets: .214/.222/.371 (0.579 OPS) with 3 HR and 11 RBI through 20 games — the power is there but a .214 average and virtually no walks is a concerning early profile.

662253.name: Andres Munoz
662253.background: The closer for the Ghent Whistlepigs and one of the most electric relievers in the AL, Andres Munoz combines a 100-mph fastball with an elite slider to generate dominant swing-and-miss rates as Seattle's primary save man. The Whistlepigs have leaned on his save totals as a major advantage in weekly CBA matchups, and his pure stuff makes him one of the most reliable closers in the game when healthy. Ghent built their bullpen strategy around having one of the best save opportunities in baseball.
662253.recentAnalysis: Munoz has had an uncharacteristically rough start for Ghent: 8 G, 7.0 IP, 9.00 ERA, 1.86 WHIP, 11 K, 2 SV — the strikeout rate remains elite but the runs have been coming in at an alarming rate in the early going.

682663.name: Agustin Ramirez
682663.background: A young catcher with enormous upside on the Delmarva Emus roster, Agustin Ramirez has drawn consistent praise for his receiving skills, arm strength, and an offensive approach that projects toward above-average production at a premium defensive position. The Emus secured him as a developmental keeper knowing that a catching prospect with his tools and athleticism is exceptionally rare. If his bat develops as projected, he could become one of the most valuable catchers in all of CBA.
682663.recentAnalysis: Ramirez has had a modest start for the Emus: .221/.287/.349 with 1 HR and 10 RBI through 21 games — still finding his footing at the MLB level, but the counting stat contributions show he's getting opportunities.

702616.name: Jackson Holliday
702616.background: One of the Portland Chinook's most exciting young investments, Jackson Holliday arrived as arguably the top prospect in all of baseball — a generational shortstop talent whose combination of elite plate discipline, advanced bat-to-ball skills, and projectable power has drawn comparisons to some of the game's all-time greats. Portland locked him up as a long-term keeper knowing his ceiling is that of a perennial MVP candidate, and the Chinook have positioned themselves perfectly to benefit from his ascent. He is the crown jewel of Portland's rebuild.
702616.recentAnalysis: Holliday is currently on the 10-day IL and has not yet recorded 2026 stats — the Chinook are eagerly awaiting the return of their most prized asset.

666969.name: Adolis Garcia
666969.background: A power outfielder for the Ghent Whistlepigs, Adolis Garcia established himself as one of the most exciting young hitters in the AL with back-to-back 25-plus HR seasons in Texas, combining impressive raw power with solid RBI production in a productive lineup. His ALCS MVP performance in 2023 showcased his ability to deliver in the biggest moments, and Ghent has counted on his power bat to contribute in the home run and RBI categories consistently. His high strikeout rate is the main knock on an otherwise compelling fantasy profile.
666969.recentAnalysis: Garcia has had a slow start for the Whistlepigs: .214/.284/.319 with 2 HR and 5 RBI through 20 games — 21 strikeouts in 81 PA is his usual profile but the power hasn't arrived yet in the early going.

673540.name: Kodai Senga
673540.background: A high-upside starter on the Portland Chinook rotation, Kodai Senga arrived from Japan with one of the most unique arsenals in baseball — featuring a ghost forkball that baffles hitters and helped him post one of the best debut seasons in recent history for the Mets. Injuries derailed his follow-up campaign, but Portland held his rights knowing a healthy Senga is a top-15 SP in all fantasy formats. The Chinook are counting on him to anchor their rotation when healthy.
673540.recentAnalysis: Senga has had a brutal start for Portland: 4 GS, 17.1 IP, 8.83 ERA, 1.90 WHIP, 22 K — the strikeout stuff is still there (11.4 K/9) but he's been hit extremely hard and the Chinook are hoping he can find the command that made him elite.

545361.name: Mike Trout
545361.background: The greatest player in West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls history and arguably the best baseball player of his generation, Mike Trout is a three-time MVP whose combination of elite power, speed, on-base skills, and defense defined the template for five-category dominance for over a decade. Injury has limited his recent availability, but West Virginia has held his rights through it all — knowing that even a partial season of Trout contributes at an elite level across every offensive category. When healthy, there is no better player in CBA.
545361.recentAnalysis: Trout has been excellent for the Pepperoni Rolls when available: .237/.424/.553 (0.980 OPS) with 7 HR, 16 RBI, 22 runs, and 21 walks through 22 games — the power and elite walk rates are fully present even if the average is running below his career norms.

625643.name: Reynaldo Lopez
625643.background: A key mid-rotation arm for the North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms, Reynaldo Lopez transformed himself from a hard-throwing reliever into a legitimate starting pitcher for Atlanta with an improved pitch mix and much better command. His ability to generate strikeouts and keep the ball on the ground has made him a reliable SP with upside as his development continues, and the Fuzzy Bottoms benefit from his placement in Atlanta's excellent pitching infrastructure. North Georgia views him as a steady contributor who can quietly win CBA pitching weeks.
625643.recentAnalysis: Lopez has been solid for the Fuzzy Bottoms: 4 GS, 20.2 IP, 2.18 ERA, 1.11 WHIP, 19 K — a 2.18 ERA is excellent even if the strikeout rate is modest, and he's been one of North Georgia's most reliable rotation pieces.

519317.name: Giancarlo Stanton
519317.background: A veteran power bat on the Sugar Land Space Cowboys roster, Giancarlo Stanton has been one of the most physically imposing hitters in baseball history — an NL MVP whose raw power generates some of the highest exit velocities and longest home runs the sport has ever measured. His injury history has been extensive, but when healthy he's a .250/30-HR threat who changes the tenor of any lineup. The Space Cowboys have kept him on their roster knowing that even a partial season of Stanton's power can swing CBA matchups.
519317.recentAnalysis: Stanton has had a modest start for Sugar Land: .240/.302/.353 with 2 HR and 10 RBI through 20 games — the enormous power that defines his value hasn't fully arrived yet in the early going.

672386.name: Alejandro Kirk
672386.background: The veteran catcher for the Syracuse Sky Chiefs, Alejandro Kirk is one of the best pure contact catchers in baseball — posting AVG figures consistently above .280 with a patient approach and gap power that makes him an above-average offensive backstop. His framing and blocking skills make him a defensive asset as well, and the Sky Chiefs have relied on his rare combination of contact hitting and catcher eligibility to anchor that roster slot. For a position that typically produces weak hitters in CBA, Kirk is a genuine standout.
672386.recentAnalysis: Kirk is currently on the 10-day IL and has appeared in just 5 games (.150 AVG, 1 HR) before the injury — Syracuse is waiting on his return to fill their catcher slot.

677587.name: Brayan Rocchio
677587.background: A slick-fielding shortstop developing into a reliable bat for the Ghent Whistlepigs, Brayan Rocchio has carved out an everyday role in Cleveland with his plus defense and improving offensive contributions. His contact-first approach, good walk rates, and developing power make him a well-rounded middle infield piece, and Ghent values his multi-category contributions at a premium defensive position. As Cleveland's lineup continues to develop around him, his counting stats and offensive profile should continue to grow.
677587.recentAnalysis: Rocchio has been solid for the Whistlepigs: .254/.342/.373 with 3 HR, 14 RBI, and 9 runs through 22 games — a well-rounded line with good walks and consistent contact from the shortstop position.

665795.name: Edward Cabrera
665795.background: A high-upside starting pitcher in the Syracuse Sky Chiefs rotation, Edward Cabrera features one of the most electric fastball-slider combinations in the Marlins' system — a mid-to-upper-90s heater paired with a wipeout breaking ball that generates elite strikeout rates when he's locked in. His career has been interrupted by injury and command inconsistency, but the Sky Chiefs held his rights knowing that when Cabrera is right he's a genuine fantasy ace. Syracuse is counting on him to deliver on that considerable potential.
665795.recentAnalysis: Cabrera has been solid for the Sky Chiefs: 4 GS, 22.2 IP, 2.38 ERA, 1.28 WHIP, 17 K — excellent run prevention in the early going, though the walk rate will need to improve to sustain these results.

695506.name: Jac Caglianone
695506.background: One of the Manhattan Mega Rats' most intriguing young position player investments, Jac Caglianone arrived as a college two-way player whose bat-first skills — enormous raw power, strong athleticism, and advanced feel for hitting — convinced the Mega Rats to prioritize him as a position player keeper. His enormous frame and elite bat speed project toward plus power at maturity, and Manhattan is betting he develops into one of the CBA's more dangerous left-handed bats. He's a long-term investment with a genuinely loud ceiling.
695506.recentAnalysis: Caglianone has had a modest debut for the Mega Rats: .270/.321/.405 with 0 HR and just 1 RBI through 19 games — the power the Mega Rats are counting on hasn't shown up yet, though contact skills look solid.

605400.name: Aaron Nola
605400.background: A veteran ace and one of the most consistent starting pitchers in the game, Aaron Nola has anchored the Philadelphia Phillies' rotation through multiple postseason runs and delivered reliable sub-3.50 ERA seasons with elite strikeout rates year after year. The Ghent Whistlepigs have benefited from his workhorse mentality and ability to pitch deep into games, providing a steady hand at the top of their CBA rotation. Nola's durability and consistency make him one of the safest picks in all of CBA.
605400.recentAnalysis: Nola has been solid for the Whistlepigs: 4 GS, 22.1 IP, 4.03 ERA, 1.30 WHIP, 24 K — the strikeout rate is right on profile (9.7 K/9) but the ERA and WHIP suggest some hard-hit ball issues in the early going.

572233.name: Christian Walker
572233.background: A first base cornerstone for the West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls, Christian Walker has been one of the most underrated first basemen in the NL — consistently posting 30-plus homer seasons with elite defense and solid on-base skills in Arizona's lineup. His combination of power, walks, and run production has made him a reliable CBA contributor year after year, and the Pepperoni Rolls have counted on his floor production to anchor their corner infield slot. Walker's consistency is as dependable as any player on their roster.
572233.recentAnalysis: Walker has been solid for West Virginia: .256/.363/.487 (0.839 OPS) with 4 HR, 16 RBI, and 11 walks through 23 games — consistent power and on-base production from a reliable veteran who has delivered for the Pepperoni Rolls.

675911.name: Spencer Strider
675911.background: The Bristol Banshees' most prized rotation investment and one of the most dominant pitchers in baseball when healthy, Spencer Strider holds the all-time MLB record for strikeouts by a right-hander in a single season — a testament to the terrifying power of his fastball-slider combination. Tommy John surgery cost him 2025, but Bristol held his rights knowing that a healthy Strider returning to form would instantly make them a CBA pitching powerhouse. The upside is as high as any pitcher in the league.
675911.recentAnalysis: Strider is on the 15-day IL and has not yet appeared in 2026 — Bristol's rotation is waiting on the return of their most important pitcher.

693307.name: Dillon Dingler
693307.background: A young catcher developing into a starting role for the Bristol Banshees, Dillon Dingler has impressed with his defensive skills and a more-than-adequate bat that makes him a useful two-way contributor at a premium position. The Banshees secured him as a developmental catcher keep knowing that his combination of arm strength, receiving, and improving offensive production gives them a long-term solution behind the plate. Bristol is banking on his power upside continuing to develop as he gets more MLB at-bats.
693307.recentAnalysis: Dingler has made a solid start for Bristol: .259/.309/.534 (0.865 OPS) with 4 HR and 14 RBI through 18 games — the power is showing up in a big way and he's quickly justifying Bristol's decision to keep him.

690986.name: Kyle Harrison
690986.background: A young left-handed starter in the West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls rotation, Kyle Harrison came up through San Francisco's system as one of the better pitching prospects in the NL — featuring a power fastball and a slider that generates elite swing-and-miss rates against both lefties and righties. His debut showed the ceiling of a mid-rotation arm with upside for more, and the Pepperoni Rolls kept him as a young keeper building toward his prime years. West Virginia has invested in his development as part of their long-term rotation plan.
690986.recentAnalysis: Harrison has been solid for the Pepperoni Rolls in his first 3 starts: 14.2 IP, 3.07 ERA, 1.09 WHIP, 15 K — good command and encouraging ratios in an early sample that bodes well for the season ahead.

669022.name: MacKenzie Gore
669022.background: A left-handed ace candidate for the Manhattan Mega Rats, MacKenzie Gore arrived with first-overall-pick pedigree and a power arsenal — mid-90s fastball, elite curveball, and developing changeup — that made him one of the most anticipated pitching prospects in years. His Washington tenure has shown both the ceiling and the inconsistency of a young pitcher still refining his command, but the Mega Rats kept him knowing when it all clicks together he's a genuine fantasy ace. Manhattan is counting on 2026 being the year it comes together.
669022.recentAnalysis: Gore has been excellent for the Mega Rats: 5 GS, 22.0 IP, 3.27 ERA, 1.09 WHIP, 31 K — a 12.7 K/9 is exactly the elite strikeout production Manhattan drafted him for, and he looks poised for a big season.

623352.name: Josh Hader
623352.background: The premier closer on the Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets roster and one of the most dominant relief pitchers of his generation, Josh Hader has converted saves at an elite rate throughout his career with a fastball-slider combination that renders lineups helpless. His ability to generate strikeouts in the ninth inning while protecting slim leads has made him a fantasy closer royalty, and the Ferrets have leaned on his save production as a weekly CBA advantage. Even into his 30s, Hader's stuff remains among the best in any bullpen.
623352.recentAnalysis: Hader is on the 15-day IL to open 2026 — the Ferrets are waiting on their closer to return and reclaim the save opportunities that make him so valuable.

691019.name: Kyle Teel
691019.background: A highly regarded catching prospect for the Bristol Banshees, Kyle Teel arrived as one of the top backstop prospects in the 2023 draft class — a left-handed hitter with outstanding plate discipline, solid catch-and-throw skills, and a mature approach at the plate well beyond his years. The Banshees secured him as a developmental keeper knowing that patient, contact-oriented catchers with his profile are extraordinarily rare in the draft. If his bat develops as projected, he could become one of the most valuable catchers in all of CBA.
691019.recentAnalysis: Teel is on the 10-day IL and has not yet recorded 2026 stats — Bristol is waiting on their top catching prospect to return and begin contributing.

682657.name: Angel Martinez
682657.background: A versatile utility piece for the Syracuse Sky Chiefs, Angel Martinez has carved out an everyday role in Cleveland with his ability to play all over the diamond — shortstop, second base, third base, and outfield — while posting a solid offensive profile with developing power and speed. The Sky Chiefs value his positional flexibility enormously, as he gives them the ability to fill any roster gap without sacrificing offensive production. As Cleveland's lineup matures around him, his counting stats should continue to grow.
682657.recentAnalysis: Martinez has been a steady contributor for Syracuse: .262/.292/.468 with 2 HR, 9 RBI, and 4 SB through 21 games — the speed is contributing and the power is showing up, making him a useful multi-category piece.

682626.name: Francisco Alvarez
682626.background: The starting catcher for the Delmarva Emus and one of the most exciting young backstops in the game, Francisco Alvarez arrived with generational catching prospect hype — a plus arm, excellent receiving skills, and raw power that made him a top-five overall prospect. His early Mets career has shown the enormous ceiling of a catcher who can hit 25-plus home runs, and the Emus secured him knowing the upside is that of a perennial All-Star at the most scarce offensive position in CBA.
682626.recentAnalysis: Alvarez has made a solid start for Delmarva: .268/.353/.527 (0.879 OPS) with 4 HR and 5 RBI through 20 games — the power the Emus are counting on is fully present and he's one of the better catcher values in early 2026.

689414.name: Liam Hicks
689414.background: A breakout hitter on the North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms roster, Liam Hicks arrived as a raw power bat with exceptional contact skills that have allowed him to translate his tools quickly at the MLB level. The Fuzzy Bottoms are among the earliest believers in his potential, having secured him as a keeper before his star had fully risen. His combination of elite bat-to-ball skills and surprising power make him one of the most exciting young bats on any CBA roster, and North Georgia is thrilled to have his rights locked up.
689414.recentAnalysis: Hicks has been absolutely on fire for the Fuzzy Bottoms: .328/.373/.552 (0.925 OPS) with 4 HR, 19 RBI, and 13 runs through 21 games — and only 5 strikeouts in 75 PA is a truly elite contact rate that makes his line even more impressive.

663158.name: Robert Suarez
663158.background: The closer for the West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls and one of the more reliable save sources in the NL, Robert Suarez features a power sinker that generates elite groundball rates and keeps the ball in the park even in big moments. His late-blooming career has produced multiple consecutive seasons of double-digit saves for San Diego, and the Pepperoni Rolls have counted on his consistency in the ninth inning to win CBA weeks in the saves and holds categories. He's a dependable, professional presence in one of the best bullpen environments in the NL.
663158.recentAnalysis: Suarez has been excellent for the Pepperoni Rolls: 9 G, 8.2 IP, 1.04 ERA, 0.92 WHIP, 10 K, 1 SV, 3 HD — elite run prevention across multiple roles in San Diego's bullpen.

667755.name: Jose Soriano
667755.background: The ace of the Ft. Meade Folksy Ferrets rotation and one of the most electrifying young starters in the AL, Jose Soriano features a triple-digit fastball and a devastating breaking ball that generate elite swing-and-miss rates throughout the lineup. Long viewed as one of the Angels' most exciting pitching prospects, he has emerged into a full-fledged fantasy ace whose pure stuff is as good as anyone in the game. The Ferrets struck gold keeping Soriano, and he's rapidly establishing himself as one of the most dominant pitchers in all of CBA.
667755.recentAnalysis: Soriano has been absolutely untouchable for Ft. Meade to open 2026: 5 GS, 32.2 IP, 0.28 ERA, 0.73 WHIP, 39 K, 5 W — one of the most dominant early-season pitching performances in recent CBA history and already the frontrunner for CBA Pitcher of the Year.

605397.name: Joe Musgrove
605397.background: A veteran rotation piece for the Ghent Whistlepigs, Joe Musgrove has been one of the most reliable starters in the NL during his San Diego tenure — a crafty right-hander with elite command and a full arsenal of secondary pitches who has delivered multiple sub-3.50 ERA seasons including a no-hitter. The Whistlepigs have relied on his durability and consistency to anchor their rotation, though his injury history has created some anxiety. When healthy, he's a dependable fantasy SP who rarely has blow-up outings.
605397.recentAnalysis: Musgrove is on the 15-day IL to start 2026 — Ghent is waiting on their veteran starter to return and provide the steady innings they count on from him.

680777.name: Ryan Jeffers
680777.background: The starting catcher for the Manhattan Mega Rats, Ryan Jeffers has developed into one of the more complete catchers in the AL — combining solid power upside with above-average defense and a patient approach that generates walks at a useful rate. His Twins tenure has produced multiple 15-plus homer seasons from behind the plate, and the Mega Rats value him as a reliable catcher who actively helps in multiple offensive categories. Catcher eligibility with his offensive profile is a genuine CBA advantage.
680777.recentAnalysis: Jeffers has made a solid start for the Mega Rats: .278/.375/.444 (0.844 OPS) with 2 HR, 12 RBI, and 10 runs through 15 games — nice production across multiple categories from the catcher position.

656730.name: Trevor Megill
656730.background: The closer for the Portland Chinook and one of their most important bullpen pieces, Trevor Megill is a power reliever who has worked his way into a save situation in a major league bullpen through elite strikeout rates and a fastball that plays up late in games. The Chinook have relied on his save production to stay competitive in the bullpen categories on a weekly basis. His ERA can be volatile but his strikeout-to-walk rates give him a legitimate path to double-digit saves in a full season.
656730.recentAnalysis: Megill has had a very rough start for the Chinook: 8 G, 7.0 IP, 10.29 ERA, 1.86 WHIP, but 4 SV — he's been hit hard but has still managed to convert saves, which is the primary contribution Portland needs from him.

691023.name: Jordan Walker
691023.background: An explosive young outfielder and one of the most exciting hitters on the Delmarva Emus roster, Jordan Walker arrived as the Cardinals' top prospect with elite raw power, plus athleticism, and a bat speed that immediately impressed at the MLB level. The Emus secured him as a keeper anticipating a breakout season where his tools would fully translate, and Walker has developed into a genuine five-category threat with home run, speed, and batting average contributions. Delmarva views him as a cornerstone of their offensive future.
