691023.recentAnalysis: Walker has been outstanding for the Emus to start 2026: .309/.367/.654 (1.025 OPS) with 8 HR, 16 RBI, 16 runs, and 3 SB through 21 games — a spectacular start that's making him one of the most valuable hitters in all of CBA.

608348.name: Carson Kelly
608348.background: The veteran catcher for the Syracuse Sky Chiefs, Carson Kelly is a dependable two-way backstop who provides solid defense and a patient, contact-oriented offensive approach that produces useful batting averages and walk rates. The Sky Chiefs rely on his professionalism and consistency at a position that can be volatile week to week in fantasy, and his ability to post .280-plus averages while providing adequate power makes him a stable floor at the C slot. He pairs well with Alejandro Kirk to give Syracuse genuine depth behind the plate.
608348.recentAnalysis: Kelly has had a strong start for the Sky Chiefs: .321/.444/.500 (0.920 OPS) with 2 HR, 10 RBI, and 10 walks through 18 games — excellent contact and plate discipline contributing nicely across multiple categories.

661395.name: Jhoan Duran
661395.background: The closer for the West Virginia Pepperoni Rolls and one of the most fearsome relievers in baseball, Jhoan Duran features a 102-mph sinker and a plus curveball that generate elite groundball rates and strikeout numbers in the highest-leverage situations. The Pepperoni Rolls have one of the best save situations in CBA, and Duran's ability to convert in the ninth inning has been a major competitive advantage. His combination of saves and dominant ratios makes him one of the most valuable relievers in the entire league.
661395.recentAnalysis: Duran has been excellent for the Pepperoni Rolls: 7 G, 6.2 IP, 1.35 ERA, 0.75 WHIP, 8 K, 5 SV — dominant ratios and save conversions are exactly what West Virginia needs from their elite closer.

682842.name: Abner Uribe
682842.background: A key bullpen piece for the North Georgia Fuzzy Bottoms, Abner Uribe features explosive triple-digit velocity and a late-breaking slider that makes him one of the most electric relievers in the NL. His role in Milwaukee's bullpen has included both save opportunities and high-leverage work, and the Fuzzy Bottoms have benefited from his holds and occasional saves in their weekly CBA matchups. His raw stuff is as good as any reliever they roster, and North Georgia is invested in his development toward a full-time closer role.
682842.recentAnalysis: Uribe has been decent for the Fuzzy Bottoms: 10 G, 9.0 IP, 4.00 ERA, 1.22 WHIP, 9 K, 2 SV, 4 HD — contributing in both saves and holds, though the ERA has been slightly elevated in the early going.

547973.name: Aroldis Chapman
547973.background: The closer for the Bristol Banshees and a legendary figure in baseball history, Aroldis Chapman has thrown the fastest pitches ever recorded — routinely hitting 105-106 mph — and has converted saves at an elite rate throughout a career that has produced multiple World Series appearances and All-Star selections. Now in the veteran chapter of his career, he brings experience, a still-dangerous fastball, and professional save conversion ability to Bristol's ninth inning. The Banshees have built their bullpen identity around his late-game dominance.

The Chinook acquired him knowing he was transitioning to a starting role, and his sinker-heavy approach translates well to longer outings where he can work through lineups without revealing a fastball-only profile. Portland is betting on his groundball dominance holding up over 5-6 innings.
605280.recentAnalysis: Holmes has been impressive in his starter conversion for the Chinook: 4 GS, 23.0 IP, 1.96 ERA, 1.09 WHIP, 16 K — excellent run prevention and a 2 wins already in what has been a very strong transition to the rotation.

681190.name: Randy Vasquez
681190.background: A young rotation piece for the Bristol Banshees, Randy Vasquez has developed quickly through the Yankees' and Padres' systems with a deceptive delivery, excellent command, and a willingness to attack hitters with multiple pitch types. The Banshees secured him as a developmental keeper knowing his stuff projects toward a solid mid-rotation profile as he enters his prime. Bristol views him as part of their long-term rotation alongside Robbie Ray and Spencer Strider, and his early results have been encouraging.
681190.recentAnalysis: Vasquez has been solid for the Banshees: 4 GS, 21.2 IP, 2.49 ERA, 1.29 WHIP, 25 K — 25 strikeouts in under 22 innings is an excellent rate and the ERA marks him as one of Bristol's more productive rotation investments so far.

518876.name: Merrill Kelly
518876.background: A veteran rotation depth piece for the Ghent Whistlepigs, Merrill Kelly has been one of Arizona's most reliable workhorses — a contact-management specialist who generates groundballs, limits walks, and consistently posts ERA figures in the mid-3.00s through elite command and pitch mix. The Whistlepigs have used him as a rotation safety net, knowing he can fill in for injured starters while posting useful ERA and WHIP numbers. His durability and professionalism make him a steady floor play.
518876.recentAnalysis: Kelly has made just 1 start so far in 2026 (5.1 IP, 3.38 ERA) — he appears to be ramping up slowly and Ghent is easing him into the rotation.

645261.name: Sandy Alcantara
645261.background: The workhorse ace of the Syracuse Sky Chiefs rotation and a former NL Cy Young winner, Sandy Alcantara is known for throwing the most innings of any pitcher in baseball — routinely completing 7-8 innings on elite pitch efficiency. Tommy John surgery cost him 2024-25, but the Sky Chiefs held his rights knowing that when healthy, his combination of innings, groundballs, and elite durability is unmatched in CBA. Syracuse needs him to be their anchor, and his return to the rotation has been one of their most important storylines.
645261.recentAnalysis: Alcantara has been outstanding for the Sky Chiefs in his return: 5 GS, 35.1 IP, 3.06 ERA, 1.05 WHIP, 23 K — the elite innings total (35.1 in 5 starts!) is exactly the durability that made him a Cy Young winner and a cornerstone for Syracuse.

693821.name: Bryce Elder
693821.background: A rotation piece and pleasant surprise for the Sugar Land Space Cowboys, Bryce Elder has developed into a reliable mid-rotation starter for Atlanta through elite command and a groundball-heavy approach that generates weak contact without premium velocity. The Space Cowboys acquired him knowing his floor is that of a solid innings-eater with useful ERA and WHIP contributions, and his early 2026 results have dramatically exceeded those expectations. Sugar Land has one of the deepest starting rotations in CBA between Elder, Burns, Messick, and McLean.
693821.recentAnalysis: Elder has been spectacular for the Space Cowboys: 4 GS, 23.1 IP, 0.77 ERA, 1.03 WHIP, 23 K — one of the best ERA marks in all of CBA early on and an enormous unexpected contribution to Sugar Land's dominant rotation.

677951.name: Bobby Witt Jr.
677951.background: The most coveted free agent in the CBA and one of the most talented young players in all of baseball, Bobby Witt Jr. is a five-category monster — combining 30-homer power, 30-plus stolen bases, a plus batting average, and elite defensive shortstop play into the most complete offensive package at any position in the game. He's currently available in CBA as a free agent, and any team that can land him in a trade or unclaimed pick adds an instant superstar to their roster. His EROSP projects him as the top player in the league.
677951.recentAnalysis: Witt has been solid but not yet explosive: .275/.330/.360 with 0 HR, 8 SB, and 8 walks through 22 games — the steals are excellent but the power is notably absent so far and the OPS is well below his usual elite level.

805779.name: Jacob Wilson
805779.background: One of the top CBA free agents and a highly-touted young shortstop with one of the best pure contact profiles among prospects in recent memory, Jacob Wilson has generated enormous buzz with elite bat-to-ball skills and a disciplined approach that projects toward .300-plus averages at maturity. Available for any CBA team to claim, he represents a significant upgrade over most rostered middle infielders and his contact ceiling is generational. Any manager who adds him is getting a potential batting title contender.
805779.recentAnalysis: Wilson has had a modest early showing: .253/.264/.368 with 2 HR, 10 RBI, and only 1 walk in 88 PA — the contact is there but the power and approach are still developing in this early MLB sample.

665489.name: Vladimir Guerrero Jr.
665489.background: The top power-hitting free agent in the CBA and one of the most dangerous right-handed hitters in baseball, Vladimir Guerrero Jr. is a perennial All-Star and MVP candidate whose combination of elite raw power, excellent contact, and RBI production makes him one of the most coveted first basemen in any fantasy format. Available to any CBA team willing to make a trade, he represents a franchise-altering pickup for a manager in need of a middle-of-the-lineup cornerstone. His EROSP puts him among the top five available players in the league.
665489.recentAnalysis: Guerrero has had a nice start: .320/.410/.413 with 1 HR, 10 RBI, and 12 walks through 21 games — elite OBP and batting average, though the power production is surprisingly quiet through the early going.

608070.name: Jose Ramirez
608070.background: A perennial CBA free agent gem and one of the most consistent five-category hitters in baseball over the past decade, Jose Ramirez is a switch-hitting third baseman who annually delivers 25-plus home runs, 20-plus steals, excellent on-base skills, and MVP-caliber overall production from the heart of Cleveland's lineup. Available for any CBA team to claim, he is arguably the best third baseman on the free agent market and represents an enormous upgrade at the position. Multiple CBA championship teams have been built around his steady excellence.
608070.recentAnalysis: Ramirez has been excellent in the early going: .229/.374/.542 (0.848 OPS) with 6 HR, 10 SB, 14 runs, and 16 walks through 23 games — the power, speed, and walks are fully live even as the batting average lags behind his usual norms.

665487.name: Fernando Tatis Jr.
665487.background: One of the most electrifying free agents in the CBA and the most physically gifted shortstop in baseball, Fernando Tatis Jr. features 35-homer power, plus stolen base ability, and a highlight-reel athleticism that makes him must-watch at all times. Available to any CBA team willing to invest their trade capital, his upside is that of the single best player in the league when he's locked in and playing at full health. His EROSP projects him in the top five CBA-available players.
665487.recentAnalysis: Tatis has had a mixed early stretch: .250/.333/.319 (0.644 OPS) with 0 HR, 5 SB, and 12 RBI through 22 games — the steals and RBI are contributing but the power that makes him elite has been completely absent in the early going.

641778.name: Eric Lauer
641778.background: A free agent starting pitcher in CBA with useful upside, Eric Lauer is a crafty left-hander whose command and secondary stuff have made him a reliable mid-rotation option during his Milwaukee and New York stints. Available for any CBA team to claim, he provides a solid floor as an innings-eater with modest K upside and good WHIP potential. He projects best as a rotation piece for a team looking to add depth rather than a frontline ace, but his consistency gives him value as a streaming or back-end option.
641778.recentAnalysis: Lauer has had a tough early stretch: 4 appearances (3 GS), 17.2 IP, 7.13 ERA, 1.47 WHIP — though 16 K shows the stuff is there and the ERA may be running hotter than his underlying numbers suggest.

663330.name: Jahmai Jones
663330.background: A versatile utility free agent in the CBA, Jahmai Jones has carved out a bench role in the majors with his ability to play multiple infield and outfield positions and provide pop-up at-bats with decent athleticism. Available for any CBA team in need of roster flexibility, he profiles best as a multi-position eligibility contributor who can cover multiple lineup slots in an emergency. He's a depth piece rather than a featured contributor.
663330.recentAnalysis: Jones has seen very limited action in 2026 with just 17 PA across 9 games (.176 AVG, 0 HR) — he's on the fringes of the roster and not generating meaningful fantasy production at this point.

683737.name: Michael Busch
683737.background: A free agent first baseman and outfielder in CBA with upside beyond his current numbers, Michael Busch arrived as a disciplined left-handed hitter from the Dodgers' system with strong plate discipline and developing power. Available for any CBA team to claim, his best profile is that of a patient hitter who draws walks and eventually contributes 15-20 home runs annually as his power develops. He projects as a useful batting average and on-base contributor if he can find consistent playing time.
683737.recentAnalysis: Busch has had a very difficult start in 2026: .164/.274/.233 with 0 HR and 3 RBI through 20 games — the walk rate is solid (10 BB) but the contact and power have been extremely poor in the early going.

695657.name: Colson Montgomery
695657.background: One of the top free agent shortstops in the CBA, Colson Montgomery is a highly-touted prospect from the White Sox system with a patient approach, developing power, and the athleticism to play premium defense at the position. Available to any CBA team willing to make the investment, he profiles as a .270-hitting shortstop with 20-HR upside once he reaches his ceiling. His elite walk rates and improving contact make him a compelling pickup for any roster in need of shortstop help.
695657.recentAnalysis: Montgomery has had a tough early stretch: .192/.271/.356 (0.717 OPS) with 4 HR and 12 RBI through 22 games — the power is present but the batting average and strikeout rate (27 K in 85 PA) are areas of concern.

650633.name: Michael King
650633.background: A free agent starting pitcher with strong upside available in the CBA, Michael King converted from one of the Yankees' best relievers into a quality starter for San Diego — immediately posting sub-3.00 ERA figures with excellent command and a diverse pitch mix. Available to any CBA team looking to add a reliable mid-rotation arm, he represents one of the best starting pitcher options in the free agent pool with a high floor and genuine SP2 upside. Any manager who adds him immediately strengthens their rotation.
650633.recentAnalysis: King has been solid in 2026: 5 GS, 23.2 IP, 2.66 ERA, 1.18 WHIP, 21 K — strong early rotation results that confirm his value as one of the better free agent pitching additions available in CBA.

671289.name: Tyler Freeman
671289.background: A free agent utility option in the CBA with multi-position flexibility, Tyler Freeman has shown flashes of a useful contact-oriented bat during his Cleveland appearances — making consistent contact with a compact swing and contributing in multiple infield positions. Available for any CBA team to claim, he profiles as a .275-hitting depth piece with limited power but solid batting average and positional flexibility. He's best deployed as a multi-eligible utility bat rather than a featured lineup piece.
671289.recentAnalysis: Freeman has seen limited action in 2026 with 37 PA across 11 games (.281 AVG, 0 HR, 4 RBI, 2 SB) — solid contact in a small sample but not generating the playing time needed for meaningful fantasy impact.

671277.name: Luis Garcia Jr.
671277.background: A free agent middle infielder in the CBA with speed and contact upside, Luis Garcia Jr. has shown the ability to contribute in multiple offensive categories during his Astros appearances — combining decent contact skills with stolen base ability and gap power from the shortstop/second base positions. Available for any CBA team looking to add infield depth, he profiles as a useful multi-position contributor with 15-SB upside and a solid batting average if given regular at-bats.
671277.recentAnalysis: Garcia has had a modest start in 2026: .221/.264/.309 with 1 HR, 12 RBI, and 1 SB through 21 games — the RBI production is a bright spot but the overall offensive line leaves room for improvement.

681217.name: Chase Silseth
681217.background: A free agent reliever in the CBA with versatile usage, Chase Silseth has shown the ability to contribute in both starting and bullpen roles with a power fastball and developing secondary stuff. Available for any CBA team looking to add bullpen depth, he profiles as a high-leverage setup arm with strikeout upside who can spot start when needed. His stuff is legitimate and any manager who adds him gets a useful multi-role arm with ceiling to grow into a featured reliever.
681217.recentAnalysis: Silseth has been effective in relief appearances in 2026: 11 G, 7.2 IP, 2.35 ERA, 1.43 WHIP, 9 K, 3 HD — solid results out of the bullpen contributing holds to the teams he's been pitching for.

664761.name: Alec Bohm
664761.background: A free agent third baseman in the CBA with significant power upside, Alec Bohm was once one of the Phillies' most highly regarded hitting prospects and has shown the ability to post .280-plus averages with 20-plus home runs in a full healthy season. Available to any CBA team looking to add third base depth or a power bat, he profiles as a .270-hitting corner infielder with 20-HR potential if he finds his swing. His track record with Philadelphia suggests he can return to that level of production.
664761.recentAnalysis: Bohm has had a very difficult start in 2026: .145/.197/.217 (0.414 OPS) with 1 HR and 10 RBI through 19 games — one of the coldest early stretches for any available free agent, though 10 RBI suggests at least some lineup opportunities.

642708.name: Amed Rosario

Available for any CBA team looking to add middle infield depth, he profiles as a .265-hitting utility player with pop and enough positional flexibility to cover multiple spots. His recent power surge makes him an intriguing add.
642708.recentAnalysis: Rosario has been solid in limited action: .262/.283/.571 (0.852 OPS) with 3 HR and 9 RBI in 46 PA across 15 games — unexpectedly strong power production makes him an appealing CBA free agent pickup.

669194.name: Ryne Nelson
669194.background: A free agent starting pitcher in CBA with solid mid-rotation upside, Ryne Nelson has shown flashes of an effective power sinker approach with Arizona that generates groundballs and keeps the ball in the park. Available for any CBA team looking to add rotation depth, he profiles as a steady innings-eater whose command and pitch mix give him a respectable ERA floor. He's a streaming-friendly option rather than a featured starter, but there's ceiling if his command sharpens.
669194.recentAnalysis: Nelson has had a tough start in 2026: 5 GS, 20.2 IP, 6.97 ERA, 1.45 WHIP — though 20 K shows the strikeout stuff is present and the ERA may be running hotter than his underlying numbers suggest.

687462.name: Spencer Horwitz
687462.background: A free agent first baseman and outfielder in CBA with elite on-base skills, Spencer Horwitz has developed into one of the most patient hitters in the AL — posting extraordinary walk rates and solid contact numbers in Toronto's lineup. Available for any CBA team looking for OBP-driven offensive production, he profiles as a .280-hitting first baseman who accumulates runs and walks at a high rate while contributing modest power. His plate discipline alone makes him a useful addition.
687462.recentAnalysis: Horwitz has had a decent start: .255/.397/.418 (0.782 OPS) with 2 HR, 5 RBI, and 11 walks in 68 PA — the walk rate is elite as always and he's been on base consistently for any team that adds him.

807713.name: Matt Shaw
807713.background: A promising free agent third baseman/shortstop in CBA, Matt Shaw arrived as the Cubs' most coveted position player prospect with a mature offensive approach, developing power, and the athleticism to handle multiple infield positions. Available for any CBA team to claim, he profiles as a .280-hitting utility infielder with 20-HR upside as he continues to develop. His combination of contact, walks, and positional flexibility make him one of the better developmental free agent options available.
807713.recentAnalysis: Shaw has had a promising early showing: .264/.310/.434 with 2 HR and 9 RBI through 19 games — solid contact and counting stat contributions from a young player finding his footing at the MLB level.

682052.name: Jacob Lopez
682052.background: A free agent starting pitcher in CBA with promising young stuff, Jacob Lopez has shown an advanced feel for pitching with solid secondary pitches and developing command that project toward a reliable mid-rotation profile. Available for any CBA team looking to add young rotation depth, he profiles as a development-track starter with the ceiling of a solid fantasy SP if his command continues to refine. He's worth a look for teams willing to roster developing arms.
682052.recentAnalysis: Lopez has had a difficult early stretch: 4 GS, 18.1 IP, 6.38 ERA, 1.96 WHIP — though 16 K shows the swing-and-miss stuff is present and a small sample may be masking his true level.

669330.name: Tyler Wells
669330.background: A free agent reliever in CBA with multi-role value, Tyler Wells has been used as both a long reliever and rotation depth piece for Baltimore — featuring solid command and a diverse pitch mix that keeps hitters off balance in multiple leverage situations. Available for any CBA team looking to add bullpen depth, he profiles as a holds-accumulating setup arm who can spot start when needed. His flexibility gives any roster an extra gear for managing pitching match-ups.
669330.recentAnalysis: Wells has been decent in relief for 2026: 10 G, 13.0 IP, 4.15 ERA, 1.23 WHIP, 16 K — solid strikeout numbers out of the bullpen with a modest ERA in high-leverage appearances.

676962.name: Ben Brown
676962.background: A free agent starter/reliever in CBA with upside, Ben Brown has shown an advanced pitch mix and promising command in his Cubs appearances — featuring a power fastball and a curveball that generates swing-and-miss rates worthy of a legitimate rotation piece. Available for any CBA team to claim, he profiles as a mid-rotation starter with strikeout upside if given consistent opportunities. His youth and stuff make him an appealing developmental add.
676962.recentAnalysis: Brown has been effective in a hybrid role: 7 appearances, 16.2 IP, 2.70 ERA, 1.08 WHIP, 15 K — solid results across flexible usage with an encouraging ERA that makes him an appealing addition.

681715.name: Heriberto Hernandez
681715.background: A free agent corner outfielder in CBA with raw power potential, Heriberto Hernandez has shown the ability to generate impressive exit velocities in his limited MLB opportunities — featuring the kind of bat speed that projects toward 20-plus home runs if given regular playing time. Available for any CBA team to claim, he profiles as a boom-or-bust power bat who will strike out at a high rate but also hit the ball extremely hard when he makes contact. A high-risk, high-reward speculative add.
681715.recentAnalysis: Hernandez has had a very difficult start: .137/.267/.176 (0.443 OPS) with 0 HR, 7 RBI, and 3 SB through 17 games — the walk rate is decent but the power and contact have been extremely poor in the early going.

807799.name: Masataka Yoshida
807799.background: A free agent DH/outfielder in CBA with one of the best pure contact profiles available, Masataka Yoshida brought elite bat-to-ball skills from Japan's NPB and has maintained that contact excellence at the MLB level — posting batting averages above .300 with excellent walk rates and modest power in Boston's lineup. Available for any CBA team looking to add a high-average bat, he profiles as a .300-hitting DH/OF with 15-HR upside and consistent on-base production. His contact skills are among the best of any available free agent.
807799.recentAnalysis: Yoshida has had a nice early stretch in limited action: .310/.474/.414 (0.888 OPS) with 0 HR, 5 RBI, and 8 walks in 38 PA across 12 games — the elite contact and OBP skills that define his profile are fully on display.

682990.name: Quinn Priester
682990.background: A free agent starting pitcher in CBA on the 15-day IL, Quinn Priester was one of Pittsburgh's top pitching prospects with a power arsenal and developing command that projected toward a mid-rotation ceiling. Available for any CBA team willing to stash him through his IL stint, he profiles as a development-track starter with legitimate upside if his command develops. He's a higher-variance pickup but the stuff is real.
682990.recentAnalysis: Priester is on the 15-day IL and has not appeared in 2026 — he's available for CBA teams willing to wait for his return before evaluating his contributions.

668885.name: Austin Martin
668885.background: A free agent utility infielder/outfielder in CBA with excellent plate discipline, Austin Martin was a top-5 overall draft pick who arrived with one of the best contact and walk profiles of any prospect in recent memory. Available for any CBA team to claim, he profiles as a .295-hitting multi-position contributor whose extraordinary patience at the plate generates walks at an elite rate. His OBP profile and positional flexibility make him one of the more underrated available free agents.
668885.recentAnalysis: Martin has been patient and productive: .295/.492/.477 (0.884 OPS) with 1 HR, 4 RBI, 11 runs, and 14 walks through 20 games — an extraordinary OBP driven by elite plate discipline, making him one of the better available free agents in CBA.

694384.name: Nolan Schanuel
694384.background: A free agent first baseman in CBA with a standout on-base profile, Nolan Schanuel arrived with one of the best college walk rates in recent draft history and has maintained that patient approach at the MLB level for the Angels. Available for any CBA team looking to add first base production, he profiles as a .260-hitting first baseman with useful walks and developing power as he continues to grow into his frame. His OBP skills alone make him worth rostering.
694384.recentAnalysis: Schanuel has had a mixed early stretch: .228/.308/.354 with 2 HR and 13 RBI through 20 games — the RBI production is decent and the walks are showing up, though the average and power leave room for improvement.

641584.name: Jake Fraley
641584.background: A free agent outfielder in CBA with solid plate discipline and gap power, Jake Fraley has produced useful batting average and on-base numbers during his stints with Tampa Bay and Cincinnati — featuring a patient approach and the ability to hit to all fields. Available for any CBA team looking for outfield depth, he profiles as a .265-hitting OF with modest power and a disciplined approach that generates walks. His best value comes in on-base and run-scoring categories.
641584.recentAnalysis: Fraley has had a modest early showing: .227/.292/.386 with 1 HR, 2 RBI, and 2 SB in 48 PA across 15 games — limited playing time and modest counting stats so far in 2026.

669003.name: Garrett Mitchell
669003.background: A free agent outfielder in CBA with elite speed and walks, Garrett Mitchell brings an exciting athletic profile — combining premium speed, plus defense in center field, and an excellent eye at the plate that generates walks at a high rate. Available for any CBA team to claim, he profiles as a .260-hitting CF with 20-SB upside and excellent OBP skills as he continues to develop his contact and power. His walk rates and speed alone make him a compelling streaming addition.
669003.recentAnalysis: Mitchell has posted some interesting numbers early: .260/.418/.420 with 1 HR, 17 RBI, 4 SB, and 16 walks through 19 games — the walk rate is exceptional and 17 RBI is a surprisingly large counting stat total.

680700.name: Richie Palacios
680700.background: A free agent utility outfielder/infielder in CBA with multi-position flexibility, Richie Palacios has shown a solid contact approach and useful speed contributions during his Cleveland and Tampa Bay appearances. Available for any CBA team looking for versatile depth, he profiles as a .280-hitting utility player with double-digit stolen base upside and the ability to cover multiple positions without being a liability. His speed and contact skills make him a useful situational piece.
680700.recentAnalysis: Palacios has been productive in limited action: .290/.359/.516 (0.855 OPS) with 1 HR, 4 RBI, and 3 SB across 14 games — a strong early line from a player getting opportunities in a new role.

686527.name: Dominic Canzone
686527.background: A free agent corner outfielder in CBA with solid power and contact balance, Dominic Canzone has shown the ability to post useful batting averages while generating 15-plus home runs in his MLB opportunities with Arizona and Seattle. Available for any CBA team looking for outfield depth with pop, he profiles as a .270-hitting corner OF with 15-HR upside who contributes in multiple categories without being a liability anywhere. A solid mid-tier free agent add.
686527.recentAnalysis: Canzone has been productive in his opportunities: .255/.302/.490 (0.810 OPS) with 2 HR, 9 RBI, and 8 runs across 18 games — solid power contributions in a limited sample from a player who could provide more if given consistent at-bats.

687637.name: Dylan Beavers
687637.background: A free agent outfielder in CBA with developing tools, Dylan Beavers was a top draft pick who has shown flashes of his plus raw power and speed combination in his early MLB opportunities with Baltimore. Available for any CBA team to claim, he profiles as a 20-HR, 15-SB upside bat still figuring out his MLB approach. The tools are legitimate and any manager who adds him is betting on his development timeline accelerating.
687637.recentAnalysis: Beavers has had a modest early showing: .212/.300/.288 with 1 HR, 6 RBI, and 1 SB through 17 games — still developing at the MLB level with the tools that convinced teams to draft him highly still waiting to fully emerge.

690953.name: Mick Abel
690953.background: A free agent starting pitcher in CBA with significant upside, Mick Abel was Philadelphia's top pitching prospect with a premium fastball, plus curveball, and advanced feel for pitching that generated top-100 prospect buzz throughout his development. Available for any CBA team to claim, he profiles as a mid-to-top-rotation starter with genuine ace upside once his command fully develops at the MLB level. His youth and stuff make him one of the more compelling developmental free agent pitcher options.

609280.name: Miguel Andujar
609280.background: A free agent corner infielder/DH in CBA with veteran pop, Miguel Andujar was once one of the most anticipated Yankees prospects and finished second in AL Rookie of the Year voting in 2018. Injuries derailed his trajectory but he remains a solid contact hitter with gap power and professional at-bats. Available for any CBA team looking for DH/corner infield depth, he profiles as a .290-hitting right-handed bat who contributes average and RBI without hurting in the other categories.

668715.name: Spencer Steer
668715.background: A free agent utility player in CBA with multi-position eligibility, Spencer Steer has shown solid power upside during his Cincinnati tenure — posting 20-plus homer seasons with a pull-heavy approach that generates hard contact at a useful rate. Available for any CBA team looking for corner infield depth, he profiles as a .255-hitting multi-position bat with 20-HR upside who contributes counting stats without being a liability in on-base categories.

663968.name: Jake Mangum
663968.background: A free agent outfielder in CBA with excellent speed and contact skills, Jake Mangum has shown the ability to maintain a high batting average and generate stolen base value throughout his professional career. Available for any CBA team looking for speed-first depth, he profiles as a .290-hitting outfielder with double-digit stolen base upside and excellent bat-to-ball skills that make him a useful high-average contributor.

664983.name: Jake McCarthy
664983.background: A free agent outfielder in CBA whose best fantasy asset is speed, Jake McCarthy has built a career on stolen base ability and gap-to-gap contact that generates runs when given regular playing time. 664983.recentAnalysis: McCarthy has had modest counting stats in his early opportunities: .200/.271/.275 with 0 HR, 3 RBI, and 5 SB across 18 games — the stolen base upside is showing up (5 SB already) even if the bat has been quiet.

663757.name: Trent Grisham
663757.background: A free agent outfielder in CBA with strong plate discipline, Trent Grisham has been one of the most patient hitters in the NL throughout his career — generating walk rates that produce elite OBP even when his batting average dips below expectations. 686668.name: Brenton Doyle
686668.background: A free agent center fielder in CBA with premium speed and defense, Brenton Doyle has shown elite athleticism and stolen base ability in Colorado's center field — combining plus-range defense with the type of blazing speed that generates big stolen base totals when he's on base. 664770.name: Nathan Lukes
664770.background: A free agent utility outfielder in CBA with solid contact skills, Nathan Lukes has made the most of his MLB opportunities with consistent line-drive contact and professional at-bats that produce useful batting average in part-time roles. Available for any CBA team looking for multi-position roster flexibility, he profiles as a .255-hitting utility bat with 12-HR upside who contributes across the board without excelling in any single category.
663837.recentAnalysis: Vierling has had limited action: .233/.250/.367 with 1 HR and 4 RBI across 15 games in 32 PA — filling a utility role with modest early production.

700932.name: Kyle Manzardo
700932.background: A free agent first baseman in CBA with developing power, Kyle Manzardo is a young left-handed hitter who has shown solid plate discipline and the raw power to project toward 20-plus homers at maturity. Available for any CBA team looking for first base depth, he profiles as a patient .265-hitting first baseman with improving power as he gains MLB experience. His walk rates and developing bat make him worth monitoring.
700932.recentAnalysis: Manzardo has had a slow start: .180/.243/.262 with 1 HR and 3 RBI through 19 games — 25 strikeouts in 68 PA is concerning, though his walk rate shows the discipline is still present.

805367.name: Chase Meidroth
805367.background: A free agent utility infielder in CBA with outstanding plate discipline, Chase Meidroth arrived with some of the best walk rates among recent prospect hitters — featuring a truly elite eye at the plate that could produce extraordinary OBP if his contact skills develop. Available for any CBA team looking for patient depth, he profiles as a .265-hitting utility player with elite walk production and developing power as he continues to mature.
805367.recentAnalysis: Meidroth has made an encouraging start: .260/.363/.342 with 1 HR, 2 RBI, and 9 walks through 20 games — the walk rate is exactly what scouts projected and he's generating on-base value even without loud counting stats.

681347.name: Mike Burrows
681347.background: A free agent starting pitcher in CBA with high strikeout upside, Mike Burrows features a power fastball and excellent secondary pitches that generate elite whiff rates in Pittsburgh's rotation. Available for any CBA team looking for a high-variance, high-ceiling rotation add, he profiles as a potential SP2 with a 10-plus K/9 ceiling but command inconsistencies that create volatility. His stuff is among the best of any pitcher in the free agent pool.
681347.recentAnalysis: Burrows has had a tough start: 5 GS, 26.2 IP, 6.75 ERA, 1.69 WHIP, 25 K — the 8.4 K/9 shows the elite stuff is present but the ERA and WHIP reflect significant command issues in the early going.

665019.name: Kody Clemens
665019.background: A free agent utility player in CBA with solid athleticism and positional flexibility, Kody Clemens has carved out a bench role by playing multiple infield and outfield positions while contributing modestly with the bat. Available for any CBA team looking for multi-position coverage, he profiles as a .255-hitting utility bat with speed upside and enough versatility to cover multiple roster spots. A depth piece whose primary value is positional flexibility.
665019.recentAnalysis: Clemens has had limited opportunities in 2026: .158/.227/.263 with 2 HR and 3 SB in 44 PA across 13 games — modest counting stats in a part-time role with the speed contributing.

696285.name: Jacob Young
696285.background: A free agent outfielder in CBA with solid speed, Jacob Young has shown the athleticism and contact skills to contribute as an everyday outfielder for Washington — bringing stolen base ability and decent batting average from the outfield position. Available for any CBA team looking for speed-focused outfield depth, he profiles as a .265-hitting OF with double-digit stolen base upside when given consistent playing time.
696285.recentAnalysis: Young has been decent in his opportunities: .254/.277/.343 with 2 HR and 8 RBI across 20 games — solid contact and modest counting stats from a player who could contribute more if given a regular starting role.

664702.name: Myles Straw
664702.background: A free agent center fielder in CBA whose primary asset is elite speed, Myles Straw has built a career on his plus-plus wheels and exceptional defense in center field — generating stolen base totals and run-scoring value through his ability to reach base and create havoc on the basepaths. Available for any CBA team specifically looking to boost stolen base totals, he's one of the fastest players in the game and can deliver double-digit steals in limited playing time.
664702.recentAnalysis: Straw has been excellent in limited action: .391/.423/.565 (1.005 OPS) with 1 HR, 3 RBI across 16 games in 26 PA — surprising power alongside his usual speed in a very small sample.

680730.name: Mitchell Parker
680730.background: A free agent left-handed pitcher in CBA with solid stuff, Mitchell Parker has shown a promising arsenal and developing command in his early Washington appearances — featuring a fastball-slider combination that generates strikeouts when his mechanics are dialed in. Available for any CBA team looking for bullpen or rotation depth, he profiles as a flexible arm who can contribute in multiple roles as he develops.
680730.recentAnalysis: Parker has been effective in relief appearances: 2 G, 5.0 IP, 5.40 ERA, 1.40 WHIP, 7 K, 1 W — the strikeout rate is encouraging even if the ERA has been slightly elevated.

669242.name: Tommy Edman
669242.background: A free agent utility player in CBA and one of the best multi-position contributors available, Tommy Edman has been one of the most valuable infielders in fantasy baseball through his combination of speed, contact, positional flexibility across the diamond, and consistent run production. On the 10-day IL, he's available for any CBA team to claim and a healthy Edman immediately upgrades any roster's speed and average categories. His multi-position eligibility is exceptionally rare.
669242.recentAnalysis: Edman is currently on the 10-day IL and has not recorded 2026 stats — CBA teams can target him for a pickup ahead of his return from the IL.

702070.name: Noah Cameron
702070.background: A free agent starting pitcher in CBA with developing stuff, Noah Cameron has worked his way into a rotation role with solid secondary pitches and improving command. Available for any CBA team looking for rotation depth, he profiles as a development-track starter who could develop into a mid-rotation piece if his command continues to sharpen.
702070.recentAnalysis: Cameron has had mixed early results: 4 GS, 20.0 IP, 5.40 ERA, 1.45 WHIP, 17 K — the strikeout rate shows the stuff is real but command issues have elevated his ERA in the early going.

680718.name: Addison Barger
680718.background: A free agent infielder in CBA on the 10-day IL, Addison Barger is a young hitter who arrived with solid tools and a developing offensive profile from Toronto's system. Available for any CBA team to monitor, a return from the IL could give him an opportunity to contribute at the MLB level as the season progresses.
680718.recentAnalysis: Barger is on the 10-day IL and has appeared in just 8 games (23 PA, .053 AVG) before the injury — not yet generating fantasy production at the MLB level.
